1	import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest'
2	import { setActivePinia, createPinia } from 'pinia'
3	import { useCheckoutStore } from '@/stores/checkout'
4	
5	vi.mock('@/api/endpoints/checkout', () => ({
6	  checkoutApi: {
7	    createCart: vi.fn(),
8	    getBestOffer: vi.fn(),
9	    applyPromo: vi.fn(),
10	    confirmCart: vi.fn(),
11	    getOrder: vi.fn(),
12	    cancelOrder: vi.fn(),
13	    getOrders: vi.fn(),
14	  },
15	}))
16	
17	import { checkoutApi } from '@/api/endpoints/checkout'
18	
19	const mockBestOffer = {
20	  subtotal: 100,
21	  discount_total: 15,
22	  total: 85,
23	  applied_promotions: [
24	    {
25	      promotion_id: 'p1',
26	      promotion_name: '10% off',
27	      promotion_type: 'pct_off',
28	      discount_amount: 10,
29	      explanation: "Applied '10% off' because workshop qualifies",
30	    },
31	    {
32	      promotion_id: 'p2',
33	      promotion_name: '$5 off',
34	      promotion_type: 'fixed_off',
35	      discount_amount: 5,
36	      explanation: "Applied '$5 off' from bundle-group — best option in group",
37	    },
38	  ],
39	}
40	
41	describe('checkoutStore', () => {
42	  beforeEach(() => {
43	    setActivePinia(createPinia())
44	    vi.clearAllMocks()
45	  })
46	
47	  it('createCart fetches best offer immediately — correction #1', async () => {
48	    vi.mocked(checkoutApi.createCart).mockResolvedValue({ id: 'cart-1', items: [{ session_id: 's1', session_title: 'Test', unit_price: 100, quantity: 1 }] })
49	    vi.mocked(checkoutApi.getBestOffer).mockResolvedValue(mockBestOffer)
50	
51	    const store = useCheckoutStore()
52	    await store.createCart([{ session_id: 's1', quantity: 1 }])
53	
54	    // Best offer must be fetched immediately after cart creation
55	    expect(checkoutApi.getBestOffer).toHaveBeenCalledWith('cart-1')
56	    expect(store.bestOffer).not.toBeNull()
57	    expect(store.bestOffer!.discount_total).toBe(15)
58	  })
59	
60	  it('bestOffer applied_promotions contain explanation text', async () => {
61	    vi.mocked(checkoutApi.createCart).mockResolvedValue({ id: 'cart-1', items: [] })
62	    vi.mocked(checkoutApi.getBestOffer).mockResolvedValue(mockBestOffer)
63	
64	    const store = useCheckoutStore()
65	    await store.createCart([])
66	
67	    expect(store.bestOffer!.applied_promotions[0].explanation).toContain('Applied')
68	    expect(store.bestOffer!.applied_promotions[1].explanation).toContain('bundle-group')
69	  })
70	
71	  it('confirmCart transitions order', async () => {
72	    vi.mocked(checkoutApi.createCart).mockResolvedValue({ id: 'cart-1', items: [] })
73	    vi.mocked(checkoutApi.getBestOffer).mockResolvedValue(mockBestOffer)
74	    vi.mocked(checkoutApi.confirmCart).mockResolvedValue({ id: 'order-1', learner_id: 'learner-1', status: 'pending', subtotal: 100, discount_total: 15, total: 85, applied_promotions: mockBestOffer.applied_promotions, items: [], currency: 'USD', created_at: '', paid_at: null, expires_at: '' })
75	
76	    const store = useCheckoutStore()
77	    store.cartId = 'cart-1'
78	    const order = await store.confirmCart()
79	    expect(order.id).toBe('order-1')
80	    expect(store.order!.status).toBe('pending')
81	  })

3	import { checkoutApi } from '@/api/endpoints/checkout'
4	
5	export interface CartItem {
6	  session_id: string
7	  session_title: string
8	  unit_price: number
9	  quantity: number
10	}
11	
12	export interface AppliedPromotion {
13	  promotion_id: string
14	  promotion_name: string
15	  discount_amount: number
16	  explanation: string
17	}
18	
19	export interface Order {
20	  id: string
21	  learner_id: string
22	  status: 'pending' | 'paid' | 'cancelled' | 'refunded'
23	  subtotal: number
24	  discount_total: number
25	  total: number
26	  currency: string
27	  applied_promotions: AppliedPromotion[]
28	  items: CartItem[]
29	  created_at: string
30	  paid_at: string | null
31	  expires_at: string
32	}
33	
34	export const useCheckoutStore = defineStore('checkout', () => {
35	  const order = ref<Order | null>(null)
36	  const loading = ref(false)
37	
38	  async function createCart(items: { session_id: string; quantity: number }[]) {
39	    loading.value = true
40	    try {
41	      order.value = await checkoutApi.createCart(items)
42	      return order.value
43	    } finally {
44	      loading.value = false
45	    }
46	  }
47	
48	  async function fetchOrder(id: string) {
49	    order.value = await checkoutApi.getOrder(id)
50	    return order.value
51	  }
52	
53	  async function cancelOrder(id: string) {
54	    order.value = await checkoutApi.cancelOrder(id)
55	    return order.value
56	  }
57	
58	  function clearCart() {
59	    order.value = null
60	  }
61	
62	  return { order, loading, createCart, fetchOrder, cancelOrder, clearCart }
63	})

13	  promotion_id: string
14	  promotion_name: string
15	  discount_amount: number
16	  explanation: string
17	}
18	
19	export interface Order {
20	  id: string
21	  learner_id: string
22	  status: 'pending' | 'paid' | 'cancelled' | 'refunded'
23	  subtotal: number
24	  discount_total: number
25	  total: number
26	  currency: string
27	  applied_promotions: AppliedPromotion[]
28	  items: CartItem[]
29	  created_at: string
30	  paid_at: string | null
31	  expires_at: string
32	}
33	
34	export const useCheckoutStore = defineStore('checkout', () => {
35	  const order = ref<Order | null>(null)
36	  const loading = ref(false)
37	
38	  async function createCart(items: { session_id: string; quantity: number }[]) {
39	    loading.value = true
40	    try {
41	      order.value = await checkoutApi.createCart(items)
42	      return order.value
43	    } finally {
44	      loading.value = false
45	    }
46	  }
47	
48	  async function fetchOrder(id: string) {
49	    order.value = await checkoutApi.getOrder(id)
50	    return order.value
51	  }
52	
53	  async function cancelOrder(id: string) {
54	    order.value = await checkoutApi.cancelOrder(id)
55	    return order.value
56	  }
57	
58	  function clearCart() {
59	    order.value = null
60	  }
61	
62	  return { order, loading, createCart, fetchOrder, cancelOrder, clearCart }
63	})

9	from app.core.config import settings
10	from app.core.exceptions import ConflictError, ForbiddenError, NotFoundError
11	from app.modules.checkout.best_offer import compute_best_offer
12	from app.modules.checkout.models import Order, OrderItem, OrderPromotion, OrderStatus
13	from app.modules.checkout.schemas import (

22	class CheckoutService:
23	    def __init__(self, db: AsyncSession) -> None:
24	        self._db = db
25	
26	    async def _get_session_price(self, session_id: uuid.UUID) -> tuple[float, str]:
27	        """Returns (price, course_id_str)."""
28	        result = await self._db.execute(
29	            select(Session, Course).join(Course, Session.course_id == Course.id).where(Session.id == session_id)
30	        )
31	        row = result.first()
32	        if not row:
33	            raise NotFoundError("Session")
34	        session, course = row
35	        return float(course.price), str(course.id)
36	
37	    async def create_cart(self, payload: CartCreate, learner_id: uuid.UUID) -> OrderResponse:
38	        subtotal = 0.0
39	        items_data: list[dict] = []
40	
41	        for item in payload.items:
42	            price, course_id = await self._get_session_price(item.session_id)
43	            line_total = price * item.quantity
44	            subtotal += line_total
45	            items_data.append({"session_id": item.session_id, "unit_price": price, "quantity": item.quantity, "course_id": course_id})
46	
47	        # Compute best-offer automatically
48	        course_ids = {i["course_id"] for i in items_data}
49	        promos_result = await self._db.execute(select(Promotion).where(Promotion.is_active == True))
50	        all_promos = promos_result.scalars().all()
51	        applied = compute_best_offer(list(all_promos), subtotal, course_ids)
52	
53	        discount_total = sum(a.discount_amount for a in applied)
54	        total = max(0.0, subtotal - discount_total)
55	        expires_at = datetime.now(UTC) + timedelta(minutes=settings.ORDER_EXPIRY_MINUTES)
56	
57	        order = Order(
58	            learner_id=learner_id,
59	            status=OrderStatus.pending,
60	            subtotal=round(subtotal, 2),
61	            discount_total=round(discount_total, 2),
62	            total=round(total, 2),
63	            expires_at=expires_at,
64	        )
65	        self._db.add(order)
66	        await self._db.flush()
67	
68	        for item in items_data:
69	            oi = OrderItem(order_id=order.id, session_id=item["session_id"], unit_price=item["unit_price"], quantity=item["quantity"])
70	            self._db.add(oi)
71	
72	        for a in applied:
73	            op = OrderPromotion(
74	                order_id=order.id,
75	                promotion_id=a.promotion.id,
76	                discount_amount=a.discount_amount,
77	                explanation=a.explanation,
78	            )
79	            self._db.add(op)
80	
81	        payment = Payment(order_id=order.id, amount=order.total, status=PaymentStatus.pending)
82	        self._db.add(payment)
83	
84	        await self._db.flush()
85	        await self._db.refresh(order)
86	
87	        return await self.get_order(order.id)
88	
89	    async def get_best_offer(self, payload: CartCreate) -> BestOfferResponse:
90	        """Dry-run best-offer calculation without persisting."""
91	        subtotal = 0.0
92	        course_ids: set[str] = set()
93	
94	        for item in payload.items:
95	            price, course_id = await self._get_session_price(item.session_id)
96	            subtotal += price * item.quantity
97	            course_ids.add(course_id)
98	
99	        promos_result = await self._db.execute(select(Promotion).where(Promotion.is_active == True))
100	        all_promos = promos_result.scalars().all()
101	        applied = compute_best_offer(list(all_promos), subtotal, course_ids)
102	        discount_total = sum(a.discount_amount for a in applied)
103	
104	        return BestOfferResponse(
105	            subtotal=round(subtotal, 2),
106	            discount_total=round(discount_total, 2),
107	            total=round(max(0.0, subtotal - discount_total), 2),
108	            applied_promotions=[
109	                AppliedPromotionDetail(
110	                    promotion_id=a.promotion.id,
111	                    promotion_name=a.promotion.name,
112	                    discount_amount=a.discount_amount,
113	                    explanation=a.explanation,
114	                )
115	                for a in applied
116	            ],
117	        )
118	
119	    async def get_order(self, order_id: uuid.UUID, caller_id: str | None = None, caller_role: str | None = None) -> OrderResponse:
120	        from sqlalchemy.orm import selectinload
121	        result = await self._db.execute(
122	            select(Order)
123	            .options(selectinload(Order.items), selectinload(Order.applied_promotions))
124	            .where(Order.id == order_id)
125	        )
126	        order = result.scalar_one_or_none()
127	        if not order:
128	            raise NotFoundError("Order")
129	        if caller_role == "learner" and caller_id and str(order.learner_id) != caller_id:
130	            raise ForbiddenError("Not your order")
131	
132	        # Fetch session titles for all items in one query
133	        session_ids = [i.session_id for i in order.items]
134	        titles: dict[uuid.UUID, str] = {}
135	        if session_ids:
136	            sess_result = await self._db.execute(
137	                select(Session).where(Session.id.in_(session_ids))
138	            )
139	            for s in sess_result.scalars().all():
140	                titles[s.id] = s.title
141	
142	        # Build response manually to include promotion details
143	        items_resp = [
144	            {"id": i.id, "session_id": i.session_id, "session_title": titles.get(i.session_id), "unit_price": float(i.unit_price), "quantity": i.quantity}
145	            for i in order.items
146	        ]
147	
148	        promo_details = []
149	        for op in order.applied_promotions:
150	            promo_result = await self._db.execute(select(Promotion).where(Promotion.id == op.promotion_id))
151	            promo = promo_result.scalar_one_or_none()
152	            promo_details.append(AppliedPromotionDetail(
153	                promotion_id=op.promotion_id,
154	                promotion_name=promo.name if promo else "Unknown",
155	                discount_amount=float(op.discount_amount),
156	                explanation=op.explanation or "",
157	            ))
158	
159	        from app.modules.checkout.schemas import OrderItemResponse
160	        return OrderResponse(
161	            id=order.id,
162	            learner_id=order.learner_id,
163	            status=order.status,
164	            subtotal=float(order.subtotal),
165	            discount_total=float(order.discount_total),
166	            total=float(order.total),
167	            currency=order.currency,
168	            created_at=order.created_at,
169	            paid_at=order.paid_at,
170	            expires_at=order.expires_at,
171	            items=[OrderItemResponse(**i) for i in items_resp],
172	            applied_promotions=promo_details,
173	        )
174	
175	    async def cancel_order(self, order_id: uuid.UUID, caller_id: str | None = None, caller_role: str | None = None) -> None:
176	        result = await self._db.execute(select(Order).where(Order.id == order_id))
177	        order = result.scalar_one_or_none()
178	        if not order:
179	            raise NotFoundError("Order")
180	        if caller_role == "learner" and caller_id and str(order.learner_id) != caller_id:
181	            raise ForbiddenError("Not your order")
182	        if order.status == OrderStatus.paid:
183	            raise ConflictError("Cannot cancel a paid order; submit a refund instead.")
184	        order.status = OrderStatus.cancelled
185	        order.closed_at = datetime.now(UTC)
186	        await self._db.flush()
187	
188	    async def list_orders(
189	        self, learner_id: uuid.UUID | None, page: int, page_size: int
190	    ) -> tuple[list[OrderResponse], int]:
191	        from sqlalchemy import func
192	        q = select(Order)
193	        if learner_id:
194	            q = q.where(Order.learner_id == learner_id)
195	        count_result = await self._db.execute(select(func.count()).select_from(q.subquery()))
196	        total = count_result.scalar_one()
197	        offset = (page - 1) * page_size
198	        result = await self._db.execute(q.order_by(Order.created_at.desc()).offset(offset).limit(page_size))
199	        orders = result.scalars().all()
200	        items = []
201	        for o in orders:
202	            items.append(await self.get_order(o.id))
203	        return items, total

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import json
4	import time
5	import uuid
6	from datetime import UTC, datetime
7	
8	from sqlalchemy import select
9	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
10	
11	from app.core.encryption import decrypt_field, encrypt_field
12	from app.core.exceptions import NotFoundError
13	from app.modules.ingestion.models import (
14	    IngestionRun, IngestionRunStatus, IngestionSource, IngestionSourceType,
15	)
16	from app.modules.ingestion.schemas import (
17	    ConnectivityResult, IngestionRunResponse, IngestionSourceCreate,
18	    IngestionSourceResponse, IngestionSourceUpdate,
19	)
20	
21	
22	class IngestionService:
23	    def __init__(self, db: AsyncSession) -> None:
24	        self._db = db
25	
26	    async def list_sources(self) -> list[IngestionSourceResponse]:
27	        result = await self._db.execute(select(IngestionSource).order_by(IngestionSource.name))
28	        return [IngestionSourceResponse.model_validate(s) for s in result.scalars().all()]
29	
30	    async def get_source(self, source_id: uuid.UUID) -> IngestionSourceResponse:
31	        result = await self._db.execute(select(IngestionSource).where(IngestionSource.id == source_id))
32	        s = result.scalar_one_or_none()
33	        if not s:
34	            raise NotFoundError("Ingestion source")
35	        return IngestionSourceResponse.model_validate(s)
36	
37	    async def create_source(self, payload: IngestionSourceCreate) -> IngestionSourceResponse:
38	        encrypted_config = encrypt_field(json.dumps(payload.config))
39	        source = IngestionSource(
40	            name=payload.name,
41	            type=payload.type,
42	            config_encrypted=encrypted_config,
43	            collection_frequency_seconds=payload.collection_frequency_seconds,
44	            concurrency_cap=payload.concurrency_cap,
45	        )
46	        self._db.add(source)
47	        await self._db.flush()
48	        await self._db.refresh(source)
49	        return IngestionSourceResponse.model_validate(source)
50	
51	    async def update_source(self, source_id: uuid.UUID, payload: IngestionSourceUpdate) -> IngestionSourceResponse:
52	        result = await self._db.execute(select(IngestionSource).where(IngestionSource.id == source_id))
53	        source = result.scalar_one_or_none()
54	        if not source:
55	            raise NotFoundError("Ingestion source")
56	        if payload.config is not None:
57	            source.config_encrypted = encrypt_field(json.dumps(payload.config))
58	        for k, v in payload.model_dump(exclude_none=True, exclude={"config"}).items():
59	            setattr(source, k, v)
60	        await self._db.flush()
61	        return IngestionSourceResponse.model_validate(source)
62	
63	    async def delete_source(self, source_id: uuid.UUID) -> None:
64	        result = await self._db.execute(select(IngestionSource).where(IngestionSource.id == source_id))
65	        source = result.scalar_one_or_none()
66	        if not source:
67	            raise NotFoundError("Ingestion source")
68	        source.is_active = False
69	        await self._db.flush()
70	
71	    async def test_connection(self, source_id: uuid.UUID) -> ConnectivityResult:
72	        result = await self._db.execute(select(IngestionSource).where(IngestionSource.id == source_id))
73	        source = result.scalar_one_or_none()
74	        if not source:
75	            raise NotFoundError("Ingestion source")
76	
77	        config = json.loads(decrypt_field(source.config_encrypted))
78	
79	        if source.type == IngestionSourceType.kafka:
80	            from app.modules.ingestion.adapters.kafka_adapter import test_connectivity
81	            ok, lat, err = test_connectivity(config)
82	        elif source.type == IngestionSourceType.cdc_mysql:
83	            from app.modules.ingestion.adapters.cdc_adapter import test_mysql_connectivity
84	            ok, lat, err = test_mysql_connectivity(config)
85	        elif source.type == IngestionSourceType.cdc_pg:
86	            from app.modules.ingestion.adapters.cdc_adapter import test_pg_connectivity
87	            ok, lat, err = test_pg_connectivity(config)
88	        elif source.type == IngestionSourceType.batch_file:
89	            from app.modules.ingestion.adapters.batch_adapter import test_connectivity
90	            ok, lat, err = test_connectivity(config)
91	        else:
92	            ok, lat, err = True, 0.0, None  # logstash uses webhook push
93	
94	        return ConnectivityResult(success=ok, latency_ms=lat, error=err)
95	
96	    async def trigger_run(self, source_id: uuid.UUID) -> IngestionRunResponse:
97	        result = await self._db.execute(select(IngestionSource).where(IngestionSource.id == source_id))
98	        source = result.scalar_one_or_none()
99	        if not source:
100	            raise NotFoundError("Ingestion source")
101	        run = await self.run_source(source)
102	        return run
103	
104	    async def run_source(self, source: IngestionSource) -> IngestionRunResponse:
105	        run = IngestionRun(source_id=source.id, started_at=datetime.now(UTC))
106	        self._db.add(run)
107	        await self._db.flush()
108	
109	        config = json.loads(decrypt_field(source.config_encrypted))
110	        rows_ingested = 0
111	        error = None
112	
113	        try:
114	            if source.type == IngestionSourceType.kafka:
115	                from app.modules.ingestion.adapters.kafka_adapter import consume_batch
116	                _, rows_ingested = consume_batch(config, source.concurrency_cap)
117	            elif source.type == IngestionSourceType.cdc_mysql:
118	                from app.modules.ingestion.adapters.cdc_adapter import pull_mysql_cdc
119	                _, rows_ingested = pull_mysql_cdc(config, source.concurrency_cap * 10)
120	            elif source.type == IngestionSourceType.cdc_pg:
121	                from app.modules.ingestion.adapters.cdc_adapter import pull_pg_cdc
122	                _, rows_ingested = pull_pg_cdc(config, source.concurrency_cap * 10)
123	            elif source.type == IngestionSourceType.logstash:
124	                # logstash delivers via webhook push; scheduled pull is a no-op
125	                rows_ingested = 0
126	            elif source.type == IngestionSourceType.batch_file:
127	                from app.modules.ingestion.adapters.batch_adapter import parse_file, filter_duplicates
128	                import redis as redis_lib
129	                from app.core.config import settings as _settings
130	                r = redis_lib.from_url(_settings.REDIS_URL)
131	                file_path = config.get("file_path", "")
132	                filename = file_path.split("/")[-1] or "data.csv"
133	                from app.core.storage import get_object_chunks
134	                bucket = config.get("bucket", _settings.MINIO_BUCKET)
135	                content = b"".join(get_object_chunks(bucket, file_path))
136	                rows = parse_file(content, filename)
137	                fresh = filter_duplicates(rows, str(source.id), r)
138	                rows_ingested = len(fresh)
139	            run.status = IngestionRunStatus.success
140	        except Exception as e:
141	            run.status = IngestionRunStatus.failed
142	            run.error_detail = str(e)
143	            error = str(e)
144	
145	        run.finished_at = datetime.now(UTC)
146	        run.rows_ingested = rows_ingested
147	        source.last_run_at = run.finished_at
148	        source.last_status = run.status.value
149	        await self._db.flush()
150	        return IngestionRunResponse.model_validate(run)
151	
152	    async def list_runs(self, source_id: uuid.UUID, limit: int = 50) -> list[IngestionRunResponse]:
153	        result = await self._db.execute(
154	            select(IngestionRun)
155	            .where(IngestionRun.source_id == source_id)
156	            .order_by(IngestionRun.started_at.desc())
157	            .limit(limit)
158	        )
159	        return [IngestionRunResponse.model_validate(r) for r in result.scalars().all()]
160	
161	    async def handle_webhook(self, source_id: uuid.UUID, payload: list[dict], api_key: str) -> IngestionRunResponse:
162	        """Handle Logstash/Flume HTTP push. Validates X-Api-Key against source config."""
163	        result = await self._db.execute(select(IngestionSource).where(IngestionSource.id == source_id))
164	        source = result.scalar_one_or_none()
165	        if not source:
166	            raise NotFoundError("Ingestion source")
167	        config = json.loads(decrypt_field(source.config_encrypted))
168	        if config.get("api_key") != api_key:
169	            from app.core.exceptions import ForbiddenError
170	            raise ForbiddenError("Invalid API key")
171	
172	        run = IngestionRun(
173	            source_id=source.id,
174	            started_at=datetime.now(UTC),
175	            rows_ingested=len(payload),
176	            status=IngestionRunStatus.success,
177	            finished_at=datetime.now(UTC),
178	        )
179	        self._db.add(run)
180	        source.last_run_at = run.finished_at
181	        source.last_status = "success"
182	        await self._db.flush()
183	        return IngestionRunResponse.model_validate(run)
184

        from sqlalchemy import func

    if current_user.role == "learner":

        result = await self._db.execute(select(Order).where(Order.id == order_id))
        order = result.scalar_one_or_none()
        if not order:
            raise NotFoundError("Order")
        if caller_role == "learner" and caller_id and str(order.learner_id) != caller_id:
            raise ForbiddenError("Not your order")
        if order.status == OrderStatus.paid:
            raise ConflictError("Cannot cancel a paid order; submit a refund instead.")
        order.status = OrderStatus.cancelled
        order.closed_at = datetime.now(UTC)
        await self._db.flush()", "new_string": "    async def cancel_order(self, order_id: uuid.UUID, caller_id: str | None = None, caller_role: str | None = None) -> OrderResponse:
        result = await self._db.execute(select(Order).where(Order.id == order_id))
        order = result.scalar_one_or_none()
        if not order:
            raise NotFoundError("Order")
        if caller_role == "learner" and caller_id and str(order.learner_id) != caller_id:
            raise ForbiddenError("Not your order")
        if order.status == OrderStatus.paid:
            raise ConflictError("Cannot cancel a paid order; submit a refund instead.")
        order.status = OrderStatus.cancelled
        order.closed_at = datetime.now(UTC)
        await self._db.flush()
        return await self.get_order(order_id)"}

check if the following issues exist and fix them if they do based on the prompt B-01 (Blocker) Recurring session contract mismatch breaks core feature
Evidence:

Backend expects nested recurrence object: backend/app/modules/sessions/schemas.py:26, backend/app/modules/sessions/schemas.py:33
Frontend sends flat payload with rrule_string: frontend/src/pages/sessions/SessionFormPage.vue:81 Impact:
Recurring scheduling (explicitly required) is likely to fail at runtime contract validation. Minimum fix:
Align payload contract end-to-end (frontend form, store, endpoint schema) and add integration tests for /api/sessions/recurring. Static verification target:
One request model shared/typed across frontend and backend contract tests.
H-01 (High) Object-level auth gap in user detail endpoint
Evidence:

Endpoint allows any authenticated user to query arbitrary user_id: backend/app/modules/users/controller.py:44
Admin flag only affects masking detail level, not ownership authorization: backend/app/modules/users/controller.py:50, backend/app/modules/users/service.py:59 Impact:
Cross-user data exposure (masked for non-admin, unmasked for admin path) and account enumeration risk. Minimum fix:
Enforce self-or-admin check before returning user detail. Static verification target:
API tests: learner cannot access other learner profile; admin can.
H-02 (High) Payment callback integrity not strongly bound to ledger values
Evidence:

Signature message excludes order_id and any persisted value check: backend/app/modules/payments/service.py:28
On signature success, payment/order are marked completed/paid: backend/app/modules/payments/service.py:57, backend/app/modules/payments/service.py:62 Impact:
Financial state may be accepted on weakly validated callback payloads. Minimum fix:
Bind signature to immutable order/payment fields and verify payload amount/currency against stored ledger before state transitions. Static verification target:
Negative tests for mismatched amount/currency/order signature context.
H-03 (High) Live-room state consistency risk in multi-worker deployment
Evidence:

Websocket room manager stores state in in-process dictionary: backend/app/modules/sessions/websocket.py:25
App starts with 4 workers: backend/docker-entrypoint.sh:42 Impact:
Participants connected to different workers may not receive peer events consistently. Minimum fix:
Use shared pub/sub state (Redis channel layer or equivalent) for room membership/signaling fanout. Static verification target:
Architecture update and tests asserting cross-worker event propagation design.
H-04 (High) Ingestion requirement mismatch for Flume and deterministic controls
Evidence:

No explicit flume type in backend enum: backend/app/modules/ingestion/models.py:12
No flume option in frontend source form: frontend/src/pages/dataops/IngestionSourceFormPage.vue:63
Connectivity fallback path returns success placeholder: backend/app/modules/ingestion/service.py:92 Impact:
Requirement fit gap for declared source support and validation reliability. Minimum fix:
Add explicit flume source type/adapter path and non-placeholder connectivity checks. Static verification target:
Source type enum/form parity tests plus adapter-level connectivity test coverage.
M-01 (Medium) Replay stream auth path bypasses inactivity timeout enforcement
Evidence:

Standard dependency checks inactivity: backend/app/core/deps.py:38
Stream token resolver checks token/blocklist but not inactivity: backend/app/modules/replays/controller.py:24, backend/app/modules/replays/controller.py:33 Impact:
Session inactivity control is inconsistently enforced across endpoints. Minimum fix:
Reuse shared user resolution dependency or apply inactivity/touch_last_seen logic in stream resolver.
M-02 (Medium) Search total_count can diverge from displayed rows with phone filter
Evidence:

total_count computed before row-level decrypted phone filtering: backend/app/modules/search/service.py:160, backend/app/modules/search/service.py:172 Impact:
Pagination/export eligibility messaging may be incorrect for users. Minimum fix:
Move phone matching into queryable indexed field strategy or reconcile count after filter stage.
M-03 (Medium) BOGO implementation is simplified and may not match business semantics
Evidence:

BOGO branch uses flat 50% subtotal simplification: backend/app/modules/checkout/best_offer.py:45 Impact:
Discount outcomes may not match expected buy-one-get-one behavior on selected workshop items. Minimum fix:
Implement quantity-aware BOGO logic scoped to eligible items.
M-04 (Medium) Audit trail gaps for access-control and payment callback actions
Evidence:

Replay access-rule mutation path has no log_audit usage in service: backend/app/modules/replays/service.py:175
Payment callback path has no callback-state audit entry, while refund paths are audited: backend/app/modules/payments/service.py:41, backend/app/modules/payments/service.py:154 Impact:
Forensic and compliance traceability is incomplete for sensitive state changes. Minimum fix:
Add audit entries for replay access rule changes and callback state transitions.
M-05 (Medium) Job lateness alerting excludes crontab schedules
Evidence:

Lateness check gates on numeric schedules only: backend/app/modules/jobs/tasks.py:238
Reconciliation is crontab-scheduled: backend/app/modules/jobs/celery_app.py:41 Impact:
Late-run detection may miss cron-based critical jobs. Minimum fix:
Extend lateness logic to evaluate crontab schedules.
M-06 (Medium) Default credentials and static development secrets are present in shipping artifacts
Evidence:

Default admin login documented: repo/README.md:34
Development secrets in compose: repo/docker-compose.yml:6, repo/docker-compose.yml:223
Startup seeds default admin user: backend/docker-entrypoint.sh:14 Impact:
Elevated hardening risk if deployment hygiene is weak. Minimum fix:
Remove/disable default credential seeding and enforce runtime secret bootstrap checks.
M-07 (Medium) Test suite reliability and coverage gaps for critical paths
Evidence:

Auth endpoint expects JSON body model: backend/app/modules/auth/controller.py:14
Multiple tests post form data instead: backend/tests/conftest.py:101, backend/tests/api/test_sessions.py:42
Lockout test expects 401 while account lock uses 423: backend/tests/api/test_auth.py:34, backend/app/core/exceptions.py:28 Impact:
Potential false confidence from contract-inconsistent tests and uncovered critical regressions. Minimum fix:
Align test request formats/status expectations with API contracts and expand coverage for blocker/high paths the codebase will be reviewed using the following promptYou are the reviewer responsible for Delivery Acceptance and Project Architecture Audit.

Work only within the current working directory which is the repo folder.

This is a static-only audit.
Do not start the project.
Do not run Docker.
Do not run tests automatically.
Do not modify code.
Do not infer runtime success from documentation alone.

Your job is to find as many material issues as possible, especially Blocker / High severity issues, while keeping all conclusions evidence-based and traceable.

[Business / Task Prompt]
{A Meridian Training Operations system with main features including class scheduling, live-and-recorded session access, offline checkout with promotions, operational search, and data ingestion for reporting. The Vue.js web interface serves five roles: Administrator (sets locations, instructors, policies), Instructor (manages sessions and attendance), Learner (books and joins sessions, watches replays), Finance Analyst (handles payments, refunds, reconciliation exports), and Data Ops (configures ingestion jobs and monitors data health). Users browse a course calendar in their chosen time zone with weekly/monthly views, recurring sessions (for example, every Tuesday and Thursday at 6:30 PM), capacity limits (such as 20 seats), and configurable buffer times (default 10 minutes between sessions). Booking follows a clear state flow from request to confirm, reschedule, or cancel; reschedules are blocked within 2 hours of start time, and learner-initiated cancellations within 24 hours can be flagged for a policy fee. When a session is live, learners enter a local “room” view; once recorded, replay tiles appear with access rules such as “only enrolled learners” and “available for 30 days after 03/27/2026.” Attendance and viewing stats are shown immediately after a session, including minutes attended, late-join counts, and replay completion rate. Checkout supports automatic best-offer calculation using stack controls (mutual exclusion by default), with examples like 10% off, $25 off orders over $200, or buy-one-get-one for selected workshops, and the UI displays traceable discount explanations on the receipt screen. Staff can run advanced search with combined filters (invoice number, learner phone, enrollment status, date range, site, instructor), save up to 20 searches per user, apply quick filters, export to CSV/Excel with a 50,000-row limit per export, and navigate fast pagination with clear “results updated” feedback.

The backend uses FastAPI to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by the Vue.js frontend, enforcing server-side validation and storing all records in PostgreSQL for local persistence. Authentication is fully offline with username and password only; passwords must be at least 12 characters with complexity rules, sessions expire after 30 minutes of inactivity, and accounts lock for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts. Sensitive fields such as phone numbers are masked in most views and encrypted at rest, and audit logs capture who changed schedules, promotions, refunds, and access controls with 90-day retention. Payments are implemented as an offline payment ledger and interface layer that can accept LAN-based “payment result callbacks” from an on-prem terminal or simulator; callbacks must pass signature verification, orders auto-close after 30 minutes unpaid, refund requests track statuses through pending to completed, and a daily reconciliation export is generated at 2:00 AM for Finance review. A built-in job scheduler orchestrates recurring tasks (attendance rollups, exports, ingestion, reconciliation) with queue-based retries using exponential backoff, and the admin console provides job throughput, latency, and success rate; when failures exceed 2% over the last hour or a job is late by 10 minutes, the system raises an in-app alert and records aggregated error logs for troubleshooting. Multi-source ingestion runs entirely on local infrastructure, supporting Kafka/Flume/Logstash streams, batch file imports, and incremental CDC pulls from MySQL/PostgreSQL sources; each source has connectivity checks, configurable collection frequency and concurrency caps, and deterministic reprocessing controls to prevent duplicate records.}

[Acceptance / Scoring Criteria (the only authority)]
{
1. Hard Gates

1.1 Documentation and static verifiability
- Whether clear startup / run / test / configuration instructions are provided
- Whether the documented entry points, configuration, and project structure appear statically consistent
- Whether the delivery provides enough static evidence for a human reviewer to attempt verification without first rewriting core code

1.2 Whether the delivered project materially deviates from the Prompt
- Whether the implementation is centered on the business goal or usage scenario described in the Prompt
- Whether there are major parts of the implementation that are only loosely related, or unrelated, to the Prompt
- Whether the project replaces, weakens, or ignores the core problem definition in the Prompt without justification

2. Delivery Completeness

2.1 Whether the delivered project fully covers the core requirements explicitly stated in the Prompt
- Whether all explicitly stated core functional requirements in the Prompt are implemented

2.2 Whether the delivered project represents a basic end-to-end deliverable from 0 to 1, rather than a partial feature, illustrative implementation, or code fragment
- Whether mock / hardcoded behavior is used in place of real logic without explanation
- Whether the project includes a complete project structure rather than scattered code or a single-file example
- Whether basic project documentation is provided, such as a README or equivalent

3. Engineering and Architecture Quality

3.1 Whether the project adopts a reasonable engineering structure and module decomposition for the scale of the problem
- Whether the project structure is clear and module responsibilities are reasonably defined
- Whether the project contains redundant or unnecessary files
- Whether the implementation is excessively piled into a single file

3.2 Whether the project shows basic maintainability and extensibility, rather than being a temporary or stacked implementation
- Whether there are obvious signs of chaotic structure or tight coupling
- Whether the core logic leaves room for extension rather than being completely hard-coded

4. Engineering Details and Professionalism

4.1 Whether the engineering details and overall shape reflect professional software practice, including but not limited to error handling, logging, validation, and API design
- Whether error handling is basically reliable and user-friendly
- Whether logs support troubleshooting rather than being random print statements or completely absent
- Whether necessary validation is present for key inputs and boundary conditions

4.2 Whether the project is organized like a real product or service, rather than remaining at the level of an example or demo
- Whether the overall deliverable resembles a real application instead of a teaching sample or demonstration-only project

5. Prompt Understanding and Requirement Fit

5.1 Whether the project accurately understands and responds to the business goal, usage scenario, and implicit constraints described in the Prompt, rather than merely implementing surface-level technical features
- Whether the core business objective in the Prompt is implemented correctly
- Whether there are obvious misunderstandings of the requirement semantics or deviations from the actual problem
- Whether key constraints in the Prompt are changed or ignored without explanation

6. Aesthetics (frontend-only / full-stack tasks only)

6.1 Whether the visual and interaction design fits the scenario and demonstrates reasonable visual quality
- Whether different functional areas of the page are visually distinguishable through background, spacing, separation, or hierarchy
- Whether the overall layout is reasonable, and whether alignment, spacing, and proportions are broadly consistent
- Whether UI elements, including text, images, and icons, render and display correctly
- Whether visual elements are consistent with the page theme and textual content, and whether there are obvious mismatches between images, illustrations, decorative elements, and the actual content
- Whether basic interaction feedback is provided, such as hover states, click states, or transitions, so users can understand the current interaction state
- Whether fonts, font sizes, colors, and icon styles are generally consistent, without obvious visual inconsistency or mixed design language
}

====================
Hard Rules (must follow)

1) Static-only audit boundary
- Perform static analysis only.
- Do not run the project, tests, Docker, or external services.
- Do not claim that a flow works at runtime unless this is directly proven by static evidence such as implementation completeness plus tests that clearly cover the flow.
- Any conclusion that depends on real runtime behavior, environment setup, network access, container orchestration, browser interaction, timing, or external integrations must be marked as:
  - Cannot Confirm Statistically
  - or Manual Verification Required

2) Prompt-to-code alignment is mandatory
- Prompt is the core constraint for the whole audit.
- You must extract the core business goal, main flows, explicit requirements, and important implicit constraints from the Prompt.
- You must compare those requirement points against the actual code, structure, interfaces, data model, tests, and documentation.
- Do not review the repository as a generic codebase only; always judge it against the Prompt.

3) Risk-first scan order is allowed and recommended
- You do not need to review in the same order as the six acceptance sections.
- You may scan in any order that improves speed and accuracy, but the final report must still be organized by the six major acceptance sections.
- Recommended scan priority:
  1. README / docs / config examples / manifests / env examples
  2. Entry points and route registration
  3. Authentication / session / token / middleware / permission guards
  4. Core business modules, services, data models, persistence layer
  5. Admin / internal / debug endpoints
  6. Test files and test configuration
  7. Frontend UI structure and visual consistency if applicable

4) Do not omit material findings, but avoid wasteful repetition
- The final report must cover every major acceptance section and every secondary requirement under it.
- If an item is not applicable, mark it as Not Applicable and explain the boundary briefly.
- If an item cannot be proven statically, mark it as Cannot Confirm Statistically and explain why.
- Merge repeated symptoms into root-cause findings where appropriate.
- Do not expand low-value duplicate issues across many files if they are caused by the same root cause.

5) Evidence must be traceable
- Every key conclusion must include precise evidence with file path + line number, such as `README.md:10` or `app/main.py:42`.
- Do not make unsupported judgments.
- Strong conclusions such as Pass / Fail / Blocker / High must not be based on vague impressions.
- Missing in the reviewed scope does not automatically mean missing in the repository; only conclude Missing when the reviewed evidence is sufficient to support that conclusion. Otherwise use Cannot Confirm Statistically.

6) Every judgment must be justified
- For every judgment such as Pass / Partial Pass / Fail / Not Applicable / Cannot Confirm Statistically, explain the reasoning briefly and concretely.
- The basis may come from:
  - alignment against the Prompt
  - alignment against the acceptance criteria
  - alignment against common engineering / architectural practice
  - code-level implementation evidence
  - static test evidence
  - documentation-to-code consistency evidence

7) Security review has priority
- Pay special attention to authentication, authorization, privilege boundaries, and data isolation.
- You must explicitly examine and assess, with evidence:
  - authentication entry points
  - route-level authorization
  - object-level authorization
  - function-level authorization
  - tenant / user data isolation
  - admin / internal / debug endpoint protection
- If a likely security risk is suggested but not fully provable statically, mark it as Suspected Risk or Cannot Confirm Statistically rather than overstating it.

8) Mock / stub / fake handling
- Mock / stub / fake behavior is not a defect by itself unless the Prompt or documentation explicitly requires real third-party integration or real production behavior.
- You must still explain:
  - how the mock behavior is implemented
  - under what conditions it is enabled
  - whether there is a risk of accidental production use
  - whether validation or safety checks can be bypassed because of it

9) Tests and logging are mandatory review dimensions
- You must statically assess unit tests, API / integration tests, and logging.
- Do not run them.
- Assess:
  - whether they exist
  - what they appear to cover
  - whether they cover core flows and important failure paths
  - whether logging categories are meaningful
  - whether there is a sensitive-data leakage risk in logs or responses

10) Static audit of test coverage is mandatory, but keep it risk-focused
- First extract the core business requirements and major risk points from the Prompt and implementation.
- Then map the most important requirement / risk points to the existing tests.
- Focus especially on:
  - core happy path
  - input validation failures
  - unauthenticated 401
  - unauthorized 403
  - not found 404
  - conflict / duplicate submission where relevant
  - object-level authorization
  - tenant / user isolation
  - pagination / sorting / filtering where relevant
  - empty data / extreme values / time fields / concurrency / repeated requests / rollback where relevant
  - sensitive log exposure
- Build coverage mapping for high-risk and core requirement areas first.
- You do not need to produce a bloated exhaustive matrix for every trivial requirement if the same root cause or same coverage gap already explains the risk.
- Still clearly state which important risks are sufficiently covered, insufficiently covered, missing, not applicable, or cannot confirm.

11) Severity rating
- Every issue must be severity-rated as:
  - Blocker
  - High
  - Medium
  - Low
- Prioritize reporting independent root-cause issues over repeated surface symptoms.
- For each issue include:
  - severity
  - conclusion
  - evidence
  - impact
  - minimum actionable fix
  - minimal verification path or manual verification suggestion if useful

12) No code modification
- This task is review only.
- Do not modify code to make the project appear to pass.
- If changes would be required, record them under Issues / Suggestions only.

====================
Output Requirements

Produce the final audit in a concise but complete report and write the consolidated report to `./.tmp/**.md`.

The final report must be organized by the six major acceptance sections in order, even if your scan order was different.

Use this structure:

1. Verdict
- Overall conclusion: Pass / Partial Pass / Fail / Cannot Confirm Statistically

2. Scope and Static Verification Boundary
- What was reviewed
- What was not reviewed
- What was intentionally not executed
- Which claims require manual verification

3. Repository / Requirement Mapping Summary
- Summarize the Prompt’s core business goal, core flows, and major constraints
- Summarize the main implementation areas you mapped against those requirements
- This section should be short and functional, not verbose

4. Section-by-section Review

For each major section and each secondary item under it, provide:
- Conclusion: Pass / Partial Pass / Fail / Not Applicable / Cannot Confirm Statistically
- Rationale: brief reasoning tied to Prompt and code
- Evidence: `file:line`
- Manual verification note only if static proof is insufficient and human follow-up is needed

5. Issues / Suggestions (Severity-Rated)

List all material issues found.
For each issue provide:
- Severity
- Title
- Conclusion
- Evidence (`file:line`)
- Impact
- Minimum actionable fix

Rules for issue listing:
- Report Blocker / High issues first
- Merge duplicate manifestations caused by the same root cause
- Do not inflate severity for style-only issues
- Do not treat lack of runtime execution as a defect
- Do not treat acceptable mocks as defects unless they violate the Prompt

6. Security Review Summary

Provide explicit conclusions, with evidence, for:
- authentication entry points
- route-level authorization
- object-level authorization
- function-level authorization
- tenant / user isolation
- admin / internal / debug protection

For each one, use:
- Pass / Partial Pass / Fail / Cannot Confirm Statistically
- plus brief evidence and reasoning

7. Tests and Logging Review

Provide separate conclusions for:
- Unit tests
- API / integration tests
- Logging categories / observability
- Sensitive-data leakage risk in logs / responses

8. Test Coverage Assessment (Static Audit)

This section is mandatory.

It must contain:

8.1 Test Overview
- Whether unit tests and API / integration tests exist
- Test framework(s)
- Test entry points
- Whether documentation provides test commands
- Evidence (`file:line`)

8.2 Coverage Mapping Table
For each core requirement or high-risk point reviewed, provide:
- Requirement / Risk Point
- Mapped Test Case(s) (`file:line`)
- Key Assertion / Fixture / Mock (`file:line`)
- Coverage Assessment: sufficient / basically covered / insufficient / missing / not applicable / cannot confirm
- Gap
- Minimum Test Addition

8.3 Security Coverage Audit
Provide coverage conclusions for:
- authentication
- route authorization
- object-level authorization
- tenant / data isolation
- admin / internal protection
For each, explain whether tests meaningfully cover the risk or whether severe defects could still remain undetected.

8.4 Final Coverage Judgment
The conclusion must be exactly one of:
- Pass
- Partial Pass
- Fail
- Cannot Confirm

Then explain the boundary clearly:
- which major risks are covered
- which uncovered risks mean the tests could still pass while severe defects remain

9. Final Notes
- Keep the report precise, evidence-based, and non-repetitive.
- Do not pad the report with generic advice.
- Do not overstate what static analysis can prove.

====================
Review Discipline

Before finalizing any strong conclusion, ask yourself:
- Is this directly supported by file:line evidence?
- Is this a static fact, or am I implying runtime behavior?
- Am I reporting a root cause, or only repeating symptoms?
- Have I judged this against the Prompt rather than generic preferences?
- If uncertain, should this be Cannot Confirm Statistically instead?

Your priority is:
1. Find real material defects
2. Keep conclusions evidence-based
3. Reduce hallucination
4. Preserve final completeness
5. Avoid unnecessary repetition

Evidence:

Backend expects nested recurrence object: backend/app/modules/sessions/schemas.py:26, backend/app/modules/sessions/schemas.py:33
Frontend sends flat payload with rrule_string: frontend/src/pages/sessions/SessionFormPage.vue:81 Impact:
Recurring scheduling (explicitly required) is likely to fail at runtime contract validation. Minimum fix:
Align payload contract end-to-end (frontend form, store, endpoint schema) and add integration tests for /api/sessions/recurring. Static verification target:
One request model shared/typed across frontend and backend contract tests.
H-01 (High) Object-level auth gap in user detail endpoint
Evidence:

Endpoint allows any authenticated user to query arbitrary user_id: backend/app/modules/users/controller.py:44
Admin flag only affects masking detail level, not ownership authorization: backend/app/modules/users/controller.py:50, backend/app/modules/users/service.py:59 Impact:
Cross-user data exposure (masked for non-admin, unmasked for admin path) and account enumeration risk. Minimum fix:
Enforce self-or-admin check before returning user detail. Static verification target:
API tests: learner cannot access other learner profile; admin can.
H-02 (High) Payment callback integrity not strongly bound to ledger values
Evidence:

Signature message excludes order_id and any persisted value check: backend/app/modules/payments/service.py:28
On signature success, payment/order are marked completed/paid: backend/app/modules/payments/service.py:57, backend/app/modules/payments/service.py:62 Impact:
Financial state may be accepted on weakly validated callback payloads. Minimum fix:
Bind signature to immutable order/payment fields and verify payload amount/currency against stored ledger before state transitions. Static verification target:
Negative tests for mismatched amount/currency/order signature context.
H-03 (High) Live-room state consistency risk in multi-worker deployment
Evidence:

Websocket room manager stores state in in-process dictionary: backend/app/modules/sessions/websocket.py:25
App starts with 4 workers: backend/docker-entrypoint.sh:42 Impact:
Participants connected to different workers may not receive peer events consistently. Minimum fix:
Use shared pub/sub state (Redis channel layer or equivalent) for room membership/signaling fanout. Static verification target:
Architecture update and tests asserting cross-worker event propagation design.
H-04 (High) Ingestion requirement mismatch for Flume and deterministic controls
Evidence:

No explicit flume type in backend enum: backend/app/modules/ingestion/models.py:12
No flume option in frontend source form: frontend/src/pages/dataops/IngestionSourceFormPage.vue:63
Connectivity fallback path returns success placeholder: backend/app/modules/ingestion/service.py:92 Impact:
Requirement fit gap for declared source support and validation reliability. Minimum fix:
Add explicit flume source type/adapter path and non-placeholder connectivity checks. Static verification target:
Source type enum/form parity tests plus adapter-level connectivity test coverage.
M-01 (Medium) Replay stream auth path bypasses inactivity timeout enforcement
Evidence:

Standard dependency checks inactivity: backend/app/core/deps.py:38
Stream token resolver checks token/blocklist but not inactivity: backend/app/modules/replays/controller.py:24, backend/app/modules/replays/controller.py:33 Impact:
Session inactivity control is inconsistently enforced across endpoints. Minimum fix:
Reuse shared user resolution dependency or apply inactivity/touch_last_seen logic in stream resolver.

Work only within the current working directory which is the repo folder.

[Business / Task Prompt]
{A Meridian Training Operations system with main features including class scheduling, live-and-recorded session access, offline checkout with promotions, operational search, and data ingestion for reporting. The Vue.js web interface serves five roles: Administrator (sets locations, instructors, policies), Instructor (manages sessions and attendance), Learner (books and joins sessions, watches replays), Finance Analyst (handles payments, refunds, reconciliation exports), and Data Ops (configures ingestion jobs and monitors data health). Users browse a course calendar in their chosen time zone with weekly/monthly views, recurring sessions (for example, every Tuesday and Thursday at 6:30 PM), capacity limits (such as 20 seats), and configurable buffer times (default 10 minutes between sessions). Booking follows a clear state flow from request to confirm, reschedule, or cancel; reschedules are blocked within 2 hours of start time, and learner-initiated cancellations within 24 hours can be flagged for a policy fee. When a session is live, learners enter a local “room” view; once recorded, replay tiles appear with access rules such as “only enrolled learners” and “available for 30 days after 03/27/2026.” Attendance and viewing stats are shown immediately after a session, including minutes attended, late-join counts, and replay completion rate. Checkout supports automatic best-offer calculation using stack controls (mutual exclusion by default), with examples like 10% off, $25 off orders over $200, or buy-one-get-one for selected workshops, and the UI displays traceable discount explanations on the receipt screen. Staff can run advanced search with combined filters (invoice number, learner phone, enrollment status, date range, site, instructor), save up to 20 searches per user, apply quick filters, export to CSV/Excel with a 50,000-row limit per export, and navigate fast pagination with clear “results updated” feedback.

The backend uses FastAPI to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by the Vue.js frontend, enforcing server-side validation and storing all records in PostgreSQL for local persistence. Authentication is fully offline with username and password only; passwords must be at least 12 characters with complexity rules, sessions expire after 30 minutes of inactivity, and accounts lock for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts. Sensitive fields such as phone numbers are masked in most views and encrypted at rest, and audit logs capture who changed schedules, promotions, refunds, and access controls with 90-day retention. Payments are implemented as an offline payment ledger and interface layer that can accept LAN-based “payment result callbacks” from an on-prem terminal or simulator; callbacks must pass signature verification, orders auto-close after 30 minutes unpaid, refund requests track statuses through pending to completed, and a daily reconciliation export is generated at 2:00 AM for Finance review. A built-in job scheduler orchestrates recurring tasks (attendance rollups, exports, ingestion, reconciliation) with queue-based retries using exponential backoff, and the admin console provides job throughput, latency, and success rate; when failures exceed 2% over the last hour or a job is late by 10 minutes, the system raises an in-app alert and records aggregated error logs for troubleshooting. Multi-source ingestion runs entirely on local infrastructure, supporting Kafka/Flume/Logstash streams, batch file imports, and incremental CDC pulls from MySQL/PostgreSQL sources; each source has connectivity checks, configurable collection frequency and concurrency caps, and deterministic reprocessing controls to prevent duplicate records.}

[Acceptance / Scoring Criteria (the only authority)]
{
1. Hard Gates

1.1 Documentation and static verifiability
- Whether clear startup / run / test / configuration instructions are provided
- Whether the documented entry points, configuration, and project structure appear statically consistent
- Whether the delivery provides enough static evidence for a human reviewer to attempt verification without first rewriting core code

1.2 Whether the delivered project materially deviates from the Prompt
- Whether the implementation is centered on the business goal or usage scenario described in the Prompt
- Whether there are major parts of the implementation that are only loosely related, or unrelated, to the Prompt
- Whether the project replaces, weakens, or ignores the core problem definition in the Prompt without justification

2. Delivery Completeness

2.1 Whether the delivered project fully covers the core requirements explicitly stated in the Prompt
- Whether all explicitly stated core functional requirements in the Prompt are implemented

2.2 Whether the delivered project represents a basic end-to-end deliverable from 0 to 1, rather than a partial feature, illustrative implementation, or code fragment
- Whether mock / hardcoded behavior is used in place of real logic without explanation
- Whether the project includes a complete project structure rather than scattered code or a single-file example
- Whether basic project documentation is provided, such as a README or equivalent

3. Engineering and Architecture Quality

3.1 Whether the project adopts a reasonable engineering structure and module decomposition for the scale of the problem
- Whether the project structure is clear and module responsibilities are reasonably defined
- Whether the project contains redundant or unnecessary files
- Whether the implementation is excessively piled into a single file

3.2 Whether the project shows basic maintainability and extensibility, rather than being a temporary or stacked implementation
- Whether there are obvious signs of chaotic structure or tight coupling
- Whether the core logic leaves room for extension rather than being completely hard-coded

4. Engineering Details and Professionalism

4.1 Whether the engineering details and overall shape reflect professional software practice, including but not limited to error handling, logging, validation, and API design
- Whether error handling is basically reliable and user-friendly
- Whether logs support troubleshooting rather than being random print statements or completely absent
- Whether necessary validation is present for key inputs and boundary conditions

4.2 Whether the project is organized like a real product or service, rather than remaining at the level of an example or demo
- Whether the overall deliverable resembles a real application instead of a teaching sample or demonstration-only project

5. Prompt Understanding and Requirement Fit

5.1 Whether the project accurately understands and responds to the business goal, usage scenario, and implicit constraints described in the Prompt, rather than merely implementing surface-level technical features
- Whether the core business objective in the Prompt is implemented correctly
- Whether there are obvious misunderstandings of the requirement semantics or deviations from the actual problem
- Whether key constraints in the Prompt are changed or ignored without explanation

6. Aesthetics (frontend-only / full-stack tasks only)

6.1 Whether the visual and interaction design fits the scenario and demonstrates reasonable visual quality
- Whether different functional areas of the page are visually distinguishable through background, spacing, separation, or hierarchy
- Whether the overall layout is reasonable, and whether alignment, spacing, and proportions are broadly consistent
- Whether UI elements, including text, images, and icons, render and display correctly
- Whether visual elements are consistent with the page theme and textual content, and whether there are obvious mismatches between images, illustrations, decorative elements, and the actual content
- Whether basic interaction feedback is provided, such as hover states, click states, or transitions, so users can understand the current interaction state
- Whether fonts, font sizes, colors, and icon styles are generally consistent, without obvious visual inconsistency or mixed design language
}

- Test entry points
- Whether documentation provides test commands
- Evidence (`file:line`)

8.2 Coverage Mapping Table
For each core requirement or high-risk point reviewed, provide:
- Requirement / Risk Point
- Mapped Test Case(s) (`file:line`)
- Key Assertion / Fixture / Mock (`file:line`)
- Coverage Assessment: sufficient / basically covered / insufficient / missing / not applicable / cannot confirm
- Gap
- Minimum Test Addition

8.3 Security Coverage Audit
Provide coverage conclusions for:
- authentication
- route authorization
- object-level authorization
- tenant / data isolation
- admin / internal protection
For each, explain whether tests meaningfully cover the risk or whether severe defects could still remain undetected.

8.4 Final Coverage Judgment
The conclusion must be exactly one of:
- Pass
- Partial Pass
- Fail
- Cannot Confirm

Then explain the boundary clearly:
- which major risks are covered
- which uncovered risks mean the tests could still pass while severe defects remain

9. Final Notes
- Keep the report precise, evidence-based, and non-repetitive.
- Do not pad the report with generic advice.
- Do not overstate what static analysis can prove.

====================
Review Discipline

Before finalizing any strong conclusion, ask yourself:
- Is this directly supported by file:line evidence?
- Is this a static fact, or am I implying runtime behavior?
- Am I reporting a root cause, or only repeating symptoms?
- Have I judged this against the Prompt rather than generic preferences?
- If uncertain, should this be Cannot Confirm Statistically instead?

Your priority is:
1. Find real material defects
2. Keep conclusions evidence-based
3. Reduce hallucination
4. Preserve final completeness
5. Avoid unnecessary repetition

6	from app.core.encryption import decrypt_field, encrypt_field
7	from app.core.exceptions import ConflictError, NotFoundError
8	from app.core.masking import mask_email, mask_phone
9	from app.core.security import hash_password
10	from app.modules.instructors.models import Instructor
11	from app.modules.users.models import User, UserRole
12	from app.modules.users.repository import UserRepository
13	from app.modules.users.schemas import UserCreate, UserDetailResponse, UserResponse, UserUpdate
14	
15	
16	def _build_response(user: User, admin: bool = False) -> UserResponse | UserDetailResponse:
17	    email_raw = decrypt_field(user.email_encrypted) if user.email_encrypted else None
18	    phone_raw = decrypt_field(user.phone_encrypted) if user.phone_encrypted else None
19	
20	    data = {
21	        "id": user.id,
22	        "username": user.username,
23	        "role": user.role,
24	        "email": mask_email(email_raw),
25	        "phone": mask_phone(phone_raw),
26	        "is_active": user.is_active,
27	        "last_login": user.last_login,
28	        "created_at": user.created_at,
29	    }
30	    if admin:
31	        return UserDetailResponse(**data, email_unmasked=email_raw, phone_unmasked=phone_raw)
32	    return UserResponse(**data)
33	
34	
35	class UserService:
36	    def __init__(self, db: AsyncSession) -> None:
37	        self._repo = UserRepository(db)
38	
39	    async def create(self, payload: UserCreate, actor_id: str | None = None) -> UserResponse:
40	        existing = await self._repo.get_by_username(payload.username)
41	        if existing:
42	            raise ConflictError(f"Username '{payload.username}' already exists.")
43	
44	        user = User(
45	            username=payload.username,
46	            password_hash=hash_password(payload.password),
47	            role=payload.role,
48	            email_encrypted=encrypt_field(payload.email) if payload.email else None,
49	            phone_encrypted=encrypt_field(payload.phone) if payload.phone else None,
50	        )
51	        user = await self._repo.create(user)
52	
53	        if payload.role == UserRole.instructor:
54	            self._repo._db.add(Instructor(user_id=user.id))
55	
56	        await log_audit(self._repo._db, actor_id or str(user.id), "user", str(user.id), "create")
57	        return _build_response(user, admin=True)
58	
59	    async def get(self, user_id: str, admin: bool = False) -> UserResponse:
60	        user = await self._repo.get_by_id(user_id)
61	        if not user:
62	            raise NotFoundError("User")
63	        return _build_response(user, admin=admin)
64	
65	    async def list(self, page: int, page_size: int, role: str | None, is_active: bool | None):
66	        users, total = await self._repo.list_users(page, page_size, role, is_active)
67	        items = [_build_response(u, admin=True) for u in users]
68	        return items, total
69	
70	    async def update(self, user_id: str, payload: UserUpdate, actor_id: str | None = None) -> UserResponse:
71	        user = await self._repo.get_by_id(user_id)
72	        if not user:
73	            raise NotFoundError("User")
74	
75	        data: dict = {}
76	        if payload.email is not None:
77	            data["email_encrypted"] = encrypt_field(payload.email)
78	        if payload.phone is not None:
79	            data["phone_encrypted"] = encrypt_field(payload.phone)
80	        if payload.is_active is not None:
81	            data["is_active"] = payload.is_active
82	        if payload.role is not None:
83	            data["role"] = payload.role
84	
85	        user = await self._repo.update(user, data)
86	
87	        if data.get("role") == UserRole.instructor:
88	            from sqlalchemy import select
89	            result = await self._repo._db.execute(
90	                select(Instructor).where(Instructor.user_id == user.id)
91	            )
92	            if not result.scalar_one_or_none():
93	                self._repo._db.add(Instructor(user_id=user.id))
94	
95	        await log_audit(self._repo._db, actor_id or user_id, "user", user_id, "update",
96	                        new_value={k: str(v) for k, v in data.items() if k not in ("email_encrypted", "phone_encrypted")})
97	        return _build_response(user, admin=True)
98	
99	    async def delete(self, user_id: str, actor_id: str | None = None) -> None:
100	        user = await self._repo.get_by_id(user_id)
101	        if not user:
102	            raise NotFoundError("User")
103	        await self._repo.soft_delete(user)
104	        await log_audit(self._repo._db, actor_id or user_id, "user", user_id, "delete")
105

27	def _verify_signature(payload: PaymentCallbackPayload) -> bool:
28	    message = f"{payload.terminal_ref}{payload.amount}{payload.timestamp}"
29	    expected = hmac.new(
30	        settings.PAYMENT_SIGNATURE_SECRET.encode(),
31	        message.encode(),
32	        hashlib.sha256,
33	    ).hexdigest()
34	    return hmac.compare_digest(expected, payload.signature)

43	
44	        result = await self._db.execute(
45	            select(Payment).where(Payment.order_id == payload.order_id)
46	        )
47	        payment = result.scalar_one_or_none()
48	        if not payment:
49	            raise NotFoundError("Payment")
50	
51	        payment.terminal_ref = payload.terminal_ref
52	        payment.callback_received_at = datetime.now(UTC)

55	
56	        if sig_ok:

60	            order = order_result.scalar_one_or_none()

3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import json
6	import logging
7	from collections import defaultdict
8	
9	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, WebSocket, WebSocketDisconnect
10	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
11	
12	from app.core.database import get_db
13	from app.core.deps import get_ws_user
14	
15	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
16	
17	router = APIRouter(tags=["Live Room WebSocket"])
18	
19	
20	class RoomConnectionManager:
21	    """In-process connection manager keyed by session_id, tracking user identity."""
22	
23	    def __init__(self) -> None:
24	        # session_id → {user_id: websocket}
25	        self._rooms: dict[str, dict[str, WebSocket]] = defaultdict(dict)
26	
27	    async def connect(self, session_id: str, user_id: str, ws: WebSocket) -> None:
28	        await ws.accept()
29	        self._rooms[session_id][user_id] = ws
30	        logger.debug("WS connected: session=%s user=%s total=%d", session_id, user_id, len(self._rooms[session_id]))
31	
32	    def disconnect(self, session_id: str, user_id: str) -> None:
33	        self._rooms[session_id].pop(user_id, None)
34	        if not self._rooms[session_id]:
35	            self._rooms.pop(session_id, None)
36	
37	    async def broadcast(self, session_id: str, message: dict, exclude_user_id: str | None = None) -> None:
38	        """Broadcast JSON to all clients in a room, optionally excluding one sender."""
39	        dead: list[str] = []
40	        for uid, ws in list(self._rooms.get(session_id, {}).items()):
41	            if uid == exclude_user_id:
42	                continue
43	            try:
44	                await ws.send_text(json.dumps(message))
45	            except Exception:
46	                dead.append(uid)
47	        for uid in dead:
48	            self.disconnect(session_id, uid)
49	
50	    async def send_to_user(self, session_id: str, user_id: str, message: dict) -> None:
51	        """Send a message to one specific user."""
52	        ws = self._rooms.get(session_id, {}).get(user_id)
53	        if ws:
54	            try:
55	                await ws.send_text(json.dumps(message))
56	            except Exception:
57	                self.disconnect(session_id, user_id)
58	
59	    def peer_ids(self, session_id: str, exclude_user_id: str) -> list[str]:
60	        return [uid for uid in self._rooms.get(session_id, {}) if uid != exclude_user_id]
61	
62	    async def send_state_snapshot(self, ws: WebSocket, session_id: str, db: AsyncSession) -> None:
63	        from sqlalchemy import select
64	        from app.modules.sessions.models import Session
65	
66	        result = await db.execute(select(Session).where(Session.id == session_id))
67	        session = result.scalar_one_or_none()
68	        if session:
69	            snapshot = {
70	                "type": "room_state",
71	                "session_status": session.status,
72	                "enrolled_count": session.enrolled_count,
73	            }
74	            await ws.send_text(json.dumps(snapshot))
75	
76	
77	room_manager = RoomConnectionManager()
78	
79	
80	@router.websocket("/api/ws/sessions/{session_id}/room")
81	async def session_room_ws(
82	    session_id: str,
83	    websocket: WebSocket,
84	    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
85	):
86	    """
87	    WebSocket endpoint for live session room.
88	    Authenticate via ?token=<access_jwt>.
89	    """
90	    # Authenticate
91	    try:
92	        user = await get_ws_user(websocket, db)
93	    except Exception:
94	        return  # get_ws_user closes the socket
95	
96	    # Verify user has access (confirmed booking, or admin/instructor)
97	    if user.role not in ("admin", "instructor"):
98	        from sqlalchemy import select, and_
99	        from app.modules.bookings.models import Booking, BookingStatus
100	        result = await db.execute(
101	            select(Booking).where(
102	                and_(
103	                    Booking.session_id == session_id,
104	                    Booking.learner_id == user.id,
105	                    Booking.status == BookingStatus.confirmed,
106	                )
107	            )
108	        )
109	        if not result.scalar_one_or_none():
110	            await websocket.close(code=4003)
111	            return
112	
113	    user_id = str(user.id)
114	    await room_manager.connect(session_id, user_id, websocket)
115	    # Send current room state on join
116	    await room_manager.send_state_snapshot(websocket, session_id, db)
117	    # Notify others that a peer joined
118	    await room_manager.broadcast(session_id, {
119	        "type": "peer_joined", "user_id": user_id, "role": user.role,
120	    }, exclude_user_id=user_id)
121	    # Tell the joining user which peers are already in the room
122	    await websocket.send_text(json.dumps({
123	        "type": "room_peers",
124	        "peers": [{"user_id": uid} for uid in room_manager.peer_ids(session_id, user_id)],
125	    }))
126	
127	    try:
128	        while True:
129	            raw = await websocket.receive_text()
130	            try:
131	                msg = json.loads(raw)
132	                msg_type = msg.get("type", "")
133	                if msg_type in ("webrtc_offer", "webrtc_answer", "webrtc_ice"):
134	                    msg["from_user_id"] = user_id
135	                    target = msg.get("target_user_id")
136	                    if target:
137	                        await room_manager.send_to_user(session_id, target, msg)
138	                    else:
139	                        await room_manager.broadcast(session_id, msg, exclude_user_id=user_id)
140	            except Exception:
141	                pass
142	    except WebSocketDisconnect:
143	        room_manager.disconnect(session_id, user_id)
144	        await room_manager.broadcast(session_id, {
145	            "type": "peer_left", "user_id": user_id, "role": user.role,
146	        })

1	"""FastAPI dependency injection: current user, role guards."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from fastapi import Depends, WebSocket
6	from fastapi.security import OAuth2PasswordBearer
7	from jose import JWTError
8	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
9	
10	from app.core.database import get_db
11	from app.core.exceptions import ForbiddenError, UnauthorizedError
12	from app.core.security import decode_token, is_inactive, is_token_blocklisted, touch_last_seen
13	
14	oauth2_scheme = OAuth2PasswordBearer(tokenUrl="/api/auth/login")
15	
16	
17	async def get_current_user(
18	    token: str = Depends(oauth2_scheme),
19	    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
20	):
21	    from app.modules.users.repository import UserRepository
22	
23	    try:
24	        payload = decode_token(token)
25	    except JWTError:
26	        raise UnauthorizedError("Invalid or expired token.")
27	
28	    if payload.get("type") != "access":
29	        raise UnauthorizedError("Invalid token type.")
30	
31	    if await is_token_blocklisted(token):
32	        raise UnauthorizedError("Token has been revoked.")
33	
34	    user_id: str = payload.get("sub")
35	    if not user_id:
36	        raise UnauthorizedError()
37	
38	    if await is_inactive(user_id):
39	        raise UnauthorizedError("Session expired due to inactivity.")
40	
41	    repo = UserRepository(db)
42	    user = await repo.get_by_id(user_id)
43	    if not user or not user.is_active:
44	        raise UnauthorizedError("User not found or inactive.")
45	
46	    await touch_last_seen(user_id)
47	    return user
48	
49	
50	def require_roles(*roles: str):
51	    """Returns a dependency that enforces role membership."""
52	
53	    async def _check(current_user=Depends(get_current_user)):
54	        if current_user.role not in roles:
55	            raise ForbiddenError()
56	        return current_user
57	
58	    return _check
59	
60

64	
65	    token = websocket.query_params.get("token")
66	    if not token:
67	        await websocket.close(code=4001)
68	        raise UnauthorizedError("Missing token.")
69	
70	    try:
71	        payload = decode_token(token)
72	    except JWTError:
73	        await websocket.close(code=4001)
74	        raise UnauthorizedError("Invalid token.")
75	
76	    if payload.get("type") != "access":
77	        await websocket.close(code=4001)
78	        raise UnauthorizedError()
79	
80	    if await is_token_blocklisted(token):
81	        await websocket.close(code=4001)
82	        raise UnauthorizedError("Token has been revoked.")
83	
84	    user_id: str = payload.get("sub")
85	
86	    if await is_inactive(user_id):

88	        raise UnauthorizedError("Session expired due to inactivity.")

90	    repo = UserRepository(db)
