54	@router.get("/sessions/{session_id}/recordings/{recording_id}/stream")
55	async def stream_recording(
56	    session_id: uuid.UUID,
57	    recording_id: uuid.UUID,
58	    request: Request,
59	    token: str = Query(...),
60	    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
61	):
62	    """
63	    Stream a recording directly from MinIO through the backend.
64	    Accepts the JWT as ?token= so it can be used as a plain <video src=>.
65	    Supports HTTP Range requests for video seeking.
66	    """
67	    user = await _resolve_stream_user(token, db)
68	    await ReplayService(db)._check_access(session_id, user.id, user.role)
69	
70	    result = await db.execute(
71	        select(SessionRecording).where(
72	            and_(
73	                SessionRecording.id == recording_id,
74	                SessionRecording.session_id == session_id,
75	                SessionRecording.upload_status == RecordingUploadStatus.ready,
76	            )
77	        )
78	    )
79	    recording = result.scalar_one_or_none()
80	    if not recording:
81	        raise NotFoundError("Recording")
82	
83	    stat = stat_object(recording.bucket_name, recording.object_storage_key)
84	    total_size = stat.size
85	    content_type = recording.mime_type or stat.content_type or "video/webm"
86	
87	    range_header = request.headers.get("Range")
88	    if range_header:
89	        # Parse "bytes=start-end"
90	        range_val = range_header.strip().replace("bytes=", "")
91	        start_str, _, end_str = range_val.partition("-")
92	        start = int(start_str) if start_str else 0
93	        end = int(end_str) if end_str else total_size - 1
94	        end = min(end, total_size - 1)
95	        length = end - start + 1
96	        return StreamingResponse(
97	            get_object_chunks(recording.bucket_name, recording.object_storage_key, offset=start, length=length),
98	            status_code=206,
99	            media_type=content_type,
100	            headers={
101	                "Content-Range": f"bytes {start}-{end}/{total_size}",
102	                "Accept-Ranges": "bytes",
103	                "Content-Length": str(length),
104	            },
105	        )
106	
107	    return StreamingResponse(
108	        get_object_chunks(recording.bucket_name, recording.object_storage_key),
109	        status_code=200,
110	        media_type=content_type,
111	        headers={
112	            "Accept-Ranges": "bytes",
113	            "Content-Length": str(total_size),
114	        },
115	    )

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import uuid
4	from datetime import UTC, datetime
5	
6	from sqlalchemy import and_, func, select, desc
7	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
8	
9	from app.core.config import settings
10	from app.core.exceptions import ForbiddenError, GoneError, NotFoundError
11	from app.core.storage import get_presigned_download_url, get_presigned_upload_url
12	from app.modules.bookings.models import Booking, BookingStatus
13	from app.modules.replays.models import (
14	    RecordingUploadStatus, ReplayAccessRule, ReplayRuleType,
15	    ReplayView, SessionRecording,
16	)
17	from app.modules.replays.schemas import (
18	    ReplayAccessRuleCreate, ReplayAccessRuleResponse, ReplayStats,
19	    ReplayViewCreate, RecordingResponse,
20	)
21	from app.modules.sessions.repository import SessionRepository
22	
23	
24	class ReplayService:
25	    def __init__(self, db: AsyncSession) -> None:
26	        self._db = db
27	        self._session_repo = SessionRepository(db)
28	
29	    async def _check_access(self, session_id: uuid.UUID, user_id: uuid.UUID, user_role: str) -> None:
30	        """Raise ForbiddenError if user cannot access the replay."""
31	        if user_role in ("admin", "instructor"):
32	            return
33	
34	        result = await self._db.execute(
35	            select(ReplayAccessRule).where(
36	                and_(ReplayAccessRule.session_id == session_id, ReplayAccessRule.is_active == True)
37	            )
38	        )
39	        rule = result.scalar_one_or_none()
40	
41	        now = datetime.now(UTC)
42	        if rule:
43	            if rule.available_from and now < rule.available_from:
44	                raise ForbiddenError("Replay is not yet available.")
45	            if rule.available_until and now > rule.available_until:
46	                raise GoneError("Replay access has expired.")
47	            if rule.rule_type == ReplayRuleType.public:
48	                return
49	
50	        # Default (no rule) and enrolled_only rule: require a confirmed booking
51	        booking_result = await self._db.execute(
52	            select(Booking).where(
53	                and_(
54	                    Booking.session_id == session_id,
55	                    Booking.learner_id == user_id,
56	                    Booking.status == BookingStatus.confirmed,
57	                )
58	            )
59	        )
60	        if not booking_result.scalar_one_or_none():
61	            raise ForbiddenError("Only enrolled learners can access this replay.")
62	
63	    async def get_replay(self, session_id: uuid.UUID, user_id: uuid.UUID, user_role: str) -> RecordingResponse:
64	        await self._check_access(session_id, user_id, user_role)
65	        result = await self._db.execute(
66	            select(SessionRecording)
67	            .where(
68	                and_(
69	                    SessionRecording.session_id == session_id,
70	                    SessionRecording.upload_status == RecordingUploadStatus.ready,
71	                )
72	            )
73	            .order_by(desc(SessionRecording.created_at))
74	            .limit(1)
75	        )
76	        recording = result.scalar_one_or_none()
77	        if not recording:
78	            raise NotFoundError("Recording")
79	
80	        resp = RecordingResponse.model_validate(recording)
81	        return resp
82	
83	    async def get_all_recordings(self, session_id: uuid.UUID, user_id: uuid.UUID, user_role: str) -> list[RecordingResponse]:
84	        await self._check_access(session_id, user_id, user_role)
85	        result = await self._db.execute(
86	            select(SessionRecording)
87	            .where(
88	                and_(
89	                    SessionRecording.session_id == session_id,
90	                    SessionRecording.upload_status == RecordingUploadStatus.ready,
91	                )
92	            )
93	            .order_by(desc(SessionRecording.created_at))
94	        )
95	        recordings = result.scalars().all()
96	        return [RecordingResponse.model_validate(r) for r in recordings]
97	
98	    async def initiate_upload(self, session_id: uuid.UUID) -> dict:
99	        """Returns a presigned PUT URL for direct upload to MinIO."""
100	        session = await self._session_repo.get(session_id)
101	        if not session:
102	            raise NotFoundError("Session")
103	
104	        object_key = f"sessions/{session_id}/recording.mp4"
105	        bucket = settings.MINIO_BUCKET_RECORDINGS
106	
107	        # Create or update recording metadata row
108	        result = await self._db.execute(
109	            select(SessionRecording).where(SessionRecording.session_id == session_id)
110	        )
111	        recording = result.scalar_one_or_none()
112	        if not recording:
113	            recording = SessionRecording(
114	                session_id=session_id,
115	                object_storage_key=object_key,
116	                bucket_name=bucket,
117	                upload_status=RecordingUploadStatus.pending,
118	            )
119	            self._db.add(recording)
120	        else:
121	            recording.object_storage_key = object_key
122	            recording.upload_status = RecordingUploadStatus.pending
123	        await self._db.flush()
124	
125	        presigned_url = get_presigned_upload_url(bucket, object_key)
126	        return {"presigned_url": presigned_url, "object_key": object_key, "bucket": bucket}
127	
128	    async def confirm_upload(self, session_id: uuid.UUID, file_size_bytes: int | None, duration_seconds: int | None) -> RecordingResponse:
129	        result = await self._db.execute(
130	            select(SessionRecording).where(SessionRecording.session_id == session_id)
131	        )
132	        recording = result.scalar_one_or_none()
133	        if not recording:
134	            raise NotFoundError("Recording")
135	        recording.upload_status = RecordingUploadStatus.ready
136	        if file_size_bytes:
137	            recording.file_size_bytes = file_size_bytes
138	        if duration_seconds:
139	            recording.duration_seconds = duration_seconds
140	        await self._db.flush()
141	        return RecordingResponse.model_validate(recording)
142	
143	    async def store_recording_direct(
144	        self, session_id: uuid.UUID, data: bytes, file_size_bytes: int, duration_seconds: int, content_type: str
145	    ) -> RecordingResponse:
146	        """Upload recording bytes directly to MinIO (used when presigned URLs are unreachable from browser)."""
147	        from app.core.storage import upload_object
148	
149	        session = await self._session_repo.get(session_id)
150	        if not session:
151	            raise NotFoundError("Session")
152	
153	        recording_id = uuid.uuid4()
154	        # Derive extension from content_type (webm or mp4)
155	        ext = "webm" if "webm" in content_type else "mp4"
156	        object_key = f"sessions/{session_id}/{recording_id}.{ext}"
157	        bucket = settings.MINIO_BUCKET_RECORDINGS
158	
159	        upload_object(bucket, object_key, data, content_type)
160	
161	        recording = SessionRecording(
162	            id=recording_id,
163	            session_id=session_id,
164	            object_storage_key=object_key,
165	            bucket_name=bucket,
166	            mime_type=content_type,
167	            upload_status=RecordingUploadStatus.ready,
168	            file_size_bytes=file_size_bytes,
169	            duration_seconds=duration_seconds,
170	        )
171	        self._db.add(recording)
172	        await self._db.flush()
173	        return RecordingResponse.model_validate(recording)
174	
175	    async def set_access_rule(self, session_id: uuid.UUID, payload: ReplayAccessRuleCreate) -> ReplayAccessRuleResponse:
176	        result = await self._db.execute(
177	            select(ReplayAccessRule).where(ReplayAccessRule.session_id == session_id)
178	        )
179	        rule = result.scalar_one_or_none()
180	        if rule:
181	            rule.rule_type = payload.rule_type
182	            rule.available_from = payload.available_from
183	            rule.available_until = payload.available_until
184	            rule.is_active = True
185	        else:
186	            rule = ReplayAccessRule(session_id=session_id, **payload.model_dump())
187	            self._db.add(rule)
188	        await self._db.flush()
189	        return ReplayAccessRuleResponse.model_validate(rule)
190	
191	    async def record_view(self, session_id: uuid.UUID, learner_id: uuid.UUID, payload: ReplayViewCreate) -> None:
192	        view = ReplayView(
193	            session_id=session_id,
194	            learner_id=learner_id,
195	            watched_seconds=payload.watched_seconds,
196	            completed=payload.completed,
197	        )
198	        self._db.add(view)
199	        await self._db.flush()
200	
201	    async def get_stats(self, session_id: uuid.UUID) -> ReplayStats:
202	        total_result = await self._db.execute(
203	            select(func.count()).where(ReplayView.session_id == session_id)
204	        )
205	        total = total_result.scalar_one()
206	
207	        unique_result = await self._db.execute(
208	            select(func.count(ReplayView.learner_id.distinct())).where(ReplayView.session_id == session_id)
209	        )
210	        unique = unique_result.scalar_one()
211	
212	        completed_result = await self._db.execute(
213	            select(func.count()).where(
214	                and_(ReplayView.session_id == session_id, ReplayView.completed == True)
215	            )
216	        )
217	        completed = completed_result.scalar_one()
218	        rate = (completed / total * 100) if total else 0.0
219	
220	        return ReplayStats(
221	            session_id=session_id,
222	            total_views=total,
223	            unique_viewers=unique,
224	            completion_rate_pct=round(rate, 1),
225	        )

1	"""
2	Best-offer promotion engine.
3	
4	Default behavior: MUTUAL EXCLUSION (per spec).
5	- null stack_group  → singleton exclusive group; only best one from all null-group promos applies
6	- same stack_group  → mutually exclusive within group (best one per group)
7	- different non-null groups → stackable across groups
8	- is_exclusive=True → cannot combine with anything, even across groups
9	"""
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import itertools
13	from dataclasses import dataclass
14	from datetime import UTC, datetime
15	from typing import Any
16	
17	from app.modules.promotions.models import Promotion, PromotionType
18	
19	
20	@dataclass
21	class AppliedPromotion:
22	    promotion: Promotion
23	    discount_amount: float
24	    explanation: str
25	
26	
27	def _calc_discount(promo: Promotion, subtotal: float, course_ids: set[str]) -> float:
28	    """Calculate the discount amount a single promotion provides on the subtotal."""
29	    # Check minimum order amount
30	    if promo.min_order_amount and subtotal < float(promo.min_order_amount):
31	        return 0.0
32	
33	    # Check course scope
34	    applies = promo.applies_to or {}
35	    if not applies.get("all"):
36	        allowed_course_ids = set(applies.get("course_ids", []))
37	        if not allowed_course_ids.intersection(course_ids):
38	            return 0.0
39	
40	    if promo.type == PromotionType.pct_off:
41	        return round(subtotal * float(promo.value) / 100, 2)
42	    elif promo.type == PromotionType.fixed_off:
43	        return round(min(float(promo.value), subtotal), 2)
44	    elif promo.type == PromotionType.bogo:
45	        # Buy one get one: 50% off the subtotal (simplification for offline context)
46	        return round(subtotal * 0.5, 2)
47	    return 0.0
48	
49	
50	def _build_explanation(promo: Promotion, discount: float) -> str:
51	    if promo.type == PromotionType.pct_off:
52	        return f"{promo.name}: {promo.value}% off → -${discount:.2f}"
53	    elif promo.type == PromotionType.fixed_off:
54	        return f"{promo.name}: ${promo.value:.2f} off → -${discount:.2f}"
55	    elif promo.type == PromotionType.bogo:
56	        return f"{promo.name}: BOGO 50% off → -${discount:.2f}"
57	    return f"{promo.name}: -${discount:.2f}"
58	
59	
60	def _group_promotions(promotions: list[Promotion]) -> dict[str | None, list[Promotion]]:
61	    """Group promotions by stack_group. None-keyed = singleton/exclusive group."""
62	    groups: dict[str | None, list[Promotion]] = {}
63	    for p in promotions:
64	        groups.setdefault(p.stack_group, []).append(p)
65	    return groups
66	
67	
68	def compute_best_offer(
69	    promotions: list[Promotion],
70	    subtotal: float,
71	    course_ids: set[str],
72	) -> list[AppliedPromotion]:
73	    """
74	    Returns the combination of promotions that maximises discount.
75	
76	    Stack rules (mutual exclusion by default):
77	    - is_exclusive promotions: tested alone only; never combined.
78	    - null stack_group: only the single best promo from the entire null pool applies.
79	    - same non-null stack_group: only the best promo from that group.
80	    - different non-null groups: one from each group may stack.
81	    """
82	    now = datetime.now(UTC)
83	    valid = [
84	        p for p in promotions
85	        if p.is_active and p.valid_from <= now <= p.valid_until
86	    ]
87	
88	    if not valid:
89	        return []
90	
91	    # --- Step 1: separate exclusive promos ---
92	    exclusive = [p for p in valid if p.is_exclusive]
93	    non_exclusive = [p for p in valid if not p.is_exclusive]
94	
95	    def _apply_single(promo: Promotion) -> list[AppliedPromotion]:
96	        d = _calc_discount(promo, subtotal, course_ids)
97	        if d <= 0:
98	            return []
99	        return [AppliedPromotion(promo, d, _build_explanation(promo, d))]
100	
101	    best: list[AppliedPromotion] = []
102	    best_total: float = 0.0
103	
104	    # --- Step 2: test each exclusive promo alone ---
105	    for p in exclusive:
106	        candidate = _apply_single(p)
107	        total = sum(a.discount_amount for a in candidate)
108	        if total > best_total:
109	            best_total = total
110	            best = candidate
111	
112	    # --- Step 3: group non-exclusive promos ---
113	    groups = _group_promotions(non_exclusive)
114	
115	    # Separate null-group (mutually exclusive by default) from named groups
116	    null_group = groups.pop(None, [])
117	    named_groups = groups  # dict[str, list[Promotion]]
118	
119	    # From the null group, pick the single best promo (mutual exclusion default)
120	    best_null: Promotion | None = None
121	    best_null_discount: float = 0.0
122	    for p in null_group:
123	        d = _calc_discount(p, subtotal, course_ids)
124	        if d > best_null_discount:
125	            best_null_discount = d
126	            best_null = p
127	
128	    # For each named group, pick the best promo from that group
129	    best_per_group: dict[str, tuple[Promotion, float]] = {}
130	    for group_name, promos in named_groups.items():
131	        best_p: Promotion | None = None
132	        best_d: float = 0.0
133	        for p in promos:
134	            d = _calc_discount(p, subtotal, course_ids)
135	            if d > best_d:
136	                best_d = d
137	                best_p = p
138	        if best_p and best_d > 0:
139	            best_per_group[group_name] = (best_p, best_d)
140	
141	    # --- Step 4: stack best-per-named-group + optional best-null ---
142	    # Build candidate combination: one from each named group (stackable) + best null
143	    candidate: list[AppliedPromotion] = []
144	    for group_name, (p, d) in best_per_group.items():
145	        candidate.append(AppliedPromotion(p, d, _build_explanation(p, d)))
146	    if best_null and best_null_discount > 0:
147	        candidate.append(AppliedPromotion(best_null, best_null_discount, _build_explanation(best_null, best_null_discount)))
148	
149	    candidate_total = sum(a.discount_amount for a in candidate)
150	    if candidate_total > best_total:
151	        best_total = candidate_total
152	        best = candidate
153	
154	    # Cap: discount cannot exceed subtotal
155	    if best_total > subtotal:
156	        # Scale down proportionally
157	        factor = subtotal / best_total
158	        best = [
159	            AppliedPromotion(a.promotion, round(a.discount_amount * factor, 2), a.explanation)
160	            for a in best
161	        ]
162	
163	    return best
164

30	def _build_count_query(filters: SearchFilters):
31	    """Simplified count query — same joins/filters as _build_base_query but no scalar subquery columns."""
32	    q = (
33	        select(func.count(Booking.id))
34	        .join(User, Booking.learner_id == User.id)
35	        .join(Session, Booking.session_id == Session.id)
36	        .join(Course, Session.course_id == Course.id)
37	        .join(Room, Session.room_id == Room.id)
38	        .join(Location, Room.location_id == Location.id)
39	        .join(Instructor, Session.instructor_id == Instructor.id)
40	    )
41	    if filters.enrollment_status:
42	        q = q.where(Booking.status == filters.enrollment_status)
43	    if filters.learner_id:
44	        q = q.where(Booking.learner_id == filters.learner_id)
45	    if filters.site_id:
46	        q = q.where(Location.id == filters.site_id)
47	    if filters.instructor_id:
48	        q = q.where(Instructor.id == filters.instructor_id)
49	    if filters.date_range:
50	        if filters.date_range.from_:
51	            q = q.where(Session.start_time >= filters.date_range.from_)
52	        if filters.date_range.to:
53	            q = q.where(Session.start_time <= filters.date_range.to)
54	    if filters.date_from:
55	        q = q.where(Session.start_time >= filters.date_from)
56	    if filters.date_to:
57	        q = q.where(Session.start_time <= filters.date_to)
58	    if filters.invoice_number:
59	        q = q.where(
60	            select(Order.id)
61	            .join(OrderItem, Order.id == OrderItem.order_id)
62	            .where(OrderItem.session_id == Booking.session_id)
63	            .where(Order.learner_id == Booking.learner_id)
64	            .where(text("CAST(orders.id AS text) ILIKE :inv").bindparams(inv=f"%{filters.invoice_number}%"))
65	            .exists()
66	        )
67	    return q
68	
69	
70	def _build_base_query(filters: SearchFilters):
71	    """Build a SQLAlchemy select against bookings joined to all related tables."""
72	    q = (
73	        select(
74	            Booking.id.label("booking_id"),
75	            Booking.session_id,
76	            Booking.learner_id,
77	            Booking.status.label("status"),
78	            User.username.label("learner_username"),
79	            User.phone_encrypted.label("phone_encrypted"),
80	            Session.start_time.label("session_date"),
81	            Course.title.label("session_title"),
82	            Location.name.label("site_name"),
83	            Location.id.label("site_id"),
84	            Instructor.id.label("instructor_id"),
85	            select(User.username)
86	            .where(User.id == Instructor.user_id)
87	            .correlate(Instructor)
88	            .scalar_subquery()
89	            .label("instructor_name"),
90	            select(Order.id)
91	            .join(OrderItem, Order.id == OrderItem.order_id)
92	            .where(OrderItem.session_id == Booking.session_id)
93	            .where(Order.learner_id == Booking.learner_id)
94	            .limit(1)
95	            .correlate(Booking)
96	            .scalar_subquery()
97	            .label("order_id"),
98	            select(Order.total)
99	            .join(OrderItem, Order.id == OrderItem.order_id)
100	            .where(OrderItem.session_id == Booking.session_id)
101	            .where(Order.learner_id == Booking.learner_id)
102	            .where(Order.status == "paid")
103	            .limit(1)
104	            .correlate(Booking)
105	            .scalar_subquery()
106	            .label("amount"),
107	        )
108	        .join(User, Booking.learner_id == User.id)
109	        .join(Session, Booking.session_id == Session.id)
110	        .join(Course, Session.course_id == Course.id)
111	        .join(Room, Session.room_id == Room.id)
112	        .join(Location, Room.location_id == Location.id)
113	        .join(Instructor, Session.instructor_id == Instructor.id)
114	    )
115	
116	    if filters.enrollment_status:
117	        q = q.where(Booking.status == filters.enrollment_status)
118	    if filters.learner_id:
119	        q = q.where(Booking.learner_id == filters.learner_id)
120	    if filters.site_id:
121	        q = q.where(Location.id == filters.site_id)
122	    if filters.instructor_id:
123	        q = q.where(Instructor.id == filters.instructor_id)
124	    if filters.date_range:
125	        if filters.date_range.from_:
126	            q = q.where(Session.start_time >= filters.date_range.from_)
127	        if filters.date_range.to:
128	            q = q.where(Session.start_time <= filters.date_range.to)
129	    if filters.date_from:
130	        q = q.where(Session.start_time >= filters.date_from)
131	    if filters.date_to:
132	        q = q.where(Session.start_time <= filters.date_to)
133	    if filters.invoice_number:
134	        # pg_trgm similarity on order UUID text
135	        q = q.where(
136	            select(Order.id)
137	            .join(OrderItem, Order.id == OrderItem.order_id)
138	            .where(OrderItem.session_id == Booking.session_id)
139	            .where(Order.learner_id == Booking.learner_id)
140	            .where(text("CAST(orders.id AS text) ILIKE :inv").bindparams(inv=f"%{filters.invoice_number}%"))
141	            .exists()
142	        )
143	
144	    return q

147	class SearchService:
148	    def __init__(self, db: AsyncSession) -> None:
149	        self._db = db
150	
151	    async def search(self, filters: SearchFilters) -> SearchResponse:
152	        t0 = time.monotonic()
153	        q = _build_base_query(filters)
154	
155	        # Phone filter (decrypt-then-match in Python — limited to feasible row count)
156	        # For large datasets a dedicated hashed phone index would be better; this covers the spec requirement
157	        phone_filter = filters.learner_phone
158	
159	        # Count total (separate query avoids scalar-subquery auto-correlation)
160	        count_result = await self._db.execute(_build_count_query(filters))
161	        total = count_result.scalar_one()
162	
163	        # Paginate
164	        offset = (filters.page - 1) * filters.page_size
165	        page_size = min(filters.page_size, settings.MAX_PAGE_SIZE)
166	        result = await self._db.execute(q.order_by(text("bookings.created_at DESC")).offset(offset).limit(page_size))
167	        rows = result.mappings().all()
168	
169	        items: list[SearchResultItem] = []
170	        for row in rows:
171	            phone_raw = decrypt_field(row["phone_encrypted"]) if row.get("phone_encrypted") else None
172	            if phone_filter and phone_raw and phone_filter not in phone_raw:
173	                continue
174	            items.append(SearchResultItem(
175	                booking_id=row["booking_id"],
176	                session_id=row["session_id"],
177	                learner_id=row["learner_id"],
178	                learner_username=row["learner_username"],
179	                learner_phone_masked=mask_phone(phone_raw),
180	                status=row["status"],
181	                session_date=row["session_date"],
182	                session_title=row["session_title"],
183	                instructor_name=row.get("instructor_name") or "",
184	                site_name=row["site_name"],
185	                order_id=row.get("order_id"),
186	                amount=float(row["amount"]) if row.get("amount") is not None else None,
187	            ))
188	
189	        elapsed = (time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000
190	
191	        # Facets
192	        facets = None
193	        if filters.include_facets:
194	            facets = await self._compute_facets(filters)
195	
196	        return SearchResponse(
197	            results=items,
198	            total_count=total,
199	            page=filters.page,
200	            page_size=page_size,
201	            has_next=(filters.page * page_size < total),
202	            query_time_ms=round(elapsed, 1),
203	            facets=facets,
204	        )
205	
206	    async def _compute_facets(self, filters: SearchFilters) -> SearchFacets:
207	        base = _build_base_query(SearchFilters(
208	            # Facets ignore pagination + enrollment_status to show all counts
209	            site_id=filters.site_id,
210	            instructor_id=filters.instructor_id,
211	            date_range=filters.date_range,
212	        ))
213	        base_sub = base.subquery()
214	
215	        # Enrollment status facet
216	        status_result = await self._db.execute(
217	            select(base_sub.c.status, func.count().label("cnt"))
218	            .group_by(base_sub.c.status)
219	        )
220	        status_facet = {row.status: row.cnt for row in status_result.all()}
221	
222	        # Site facet
223	        site_result = await self._db.execute(
224	            select(base_sub.c.site_id, base_sub.c.site_name, func.count().label("cnt"))
225	            .group_by(base_sub.c.site_id, base_sub.c.site_name)
226	        )
227	        site_facet = [FacetCount(id=str(r.site_id), name=r.site_name, count=r.cnt) for r in site_result.all()]
228	
229	        # Instructor facet
230	        instr_result = await self._db.execute(
231	            select(base_sub.c.instructor_id, base_sub.c.instructor_name, func.count().label("cnt"))
232	            .group_by(base_sub.c.instructor_id, base_sub.c.instructor_name)
233	        )
234	        instr_facet = [FacetCount(id=str(r.instructor_id), name=r.instructor_name or "", count=r.cnt) for r in instr_result.all()]
235	
236	        return SearchFacets(enrollment_status=status_facet, site=site_facet, instructor=instr_facet)
237	
238	    async def export(self, filters: SearchFilters, fmt: str) -> bytes:
239	        if filters.page_size > settings.EXPORT_ROW_LIMIT:
240	            filters.page_size = settings.EXPORT_ROW_LIMIT
241	        filters.page = 1
242	
243	        # Check total first
244	        q = _build_base_query(filters)
245	        count_result = await self._db.execute(_build_count_query(filters))
246	        total = count_result.scalar_one()
247	        if total > settings.EXPORT_ROW_LIMIT:
248	            raise AppError(f"Export exceeds {settings.EXPORT_ROW_LIMIT} rows. Apply narrower filters.", 400)
249	
250	        result = await self._db.execute(q.order_by(text("bookings.created_at DESC")).limit(settings.EXPORT_ROW_LIMIT))
251	        rows = result.mappings().all()
252	
253	        headers = ["booking_id", "session_id", "learner_id", "learner_username", "enrollment_status",
254	                   "session_start", "course_title", "instructor_name", "site_name", "order_id"]
255	
256	        if fmt == "excel":
257	            import openpyxl
258	            wb = openpyxl.Workbook()
259	            ws = wb.active
260	            ws.append(headers)
261	            for row in rows:
262	                ws.append([str(row.get(h) or "") for h in headers])
263	            buf = io.BytesIO()
264	            wb.save(buf)
265	            return buf.getvalue()
266	        else:
267	            buf = io.StringIO()
268	            writer = csv.writer(buf)
269	            writer.writerow(headers)
270	            for row in rows:
271	                writer.writerow([str(row.get(h) or "") for h in headers])
272	            return buf.getvalue().encode()
273	
274	    async def save_search(self, user_id: uuid.UUID, payload: SavedSearchCreate) -> SavedSearchResponse:
275	        from sqlalchemy import func as sqlfunc
276	        count_result = await self._db.execute(
277	            select(func.count()).where(SavedSearch.user_id == user_id)
278	        )
279	        count = count_result.scalar_one()
280	        if count >= 20:
281	            raise AppError("Maximum of 20 saved searches per user reached.", 400)
282	
283	        ss = SavedSearch(user_id=user_id, name=payload.name, filters_json=payload.filters)
284	        self._db.add(ss)
285	        await self._db.flush()
286	        return SavedSearchResponse.model_validate(ss)
287	
288	    async def list_saved_searches(self, user_id: uuid.UUID) -> list[SavedSearchResponse]:
289	        result = await self._db.execute(
290	            select(SavedSearch).where(SavedSearch.user_id == user_id).order_by(SavedSearch.created_at.desc())
291	        )

logging from collections import defaultdict from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, WebSocket, WebSocketDisconnect from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession from app.core.database import get_db from app.core.deps import get_ws_user logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) router = APIRouter(tags=["Live Room WebSocket"]) class RoomConnectionManager: """In-process connection manager keyed by session_id, tracking user identity.""" def __init__(self) -> None: # session_id → {user_id: websocket} self._rooms: dict[str, dict[str, WebSocket]] = defaultdict(dict) async def connect(self, session_id: str, user_id: str, ws: WebSocket) -> None: await ws.accept() self._rooms[session_id][user_id] = ws logger.debug("WS connected: session=%s user=%s total=%d", session_id, user_id, len(self._rooms[session_id])) def disconnect(self, session_id: str, user_id: str) -> None: self._rooms[session_id].pop(user_id, None) if not self._rooms[session_id]: self._rooms.pop(session_id, None) async def broadcast(self, session_id: str, message: dict, exclude_user_id: str | None = None) -> None: """Broadcast JSON to all clients in a room, optionally excluding one sender.""" dead: list[str] = [] for uid, ws in list(self._rooms.get(session_id, {}).items()): if uid == exclude_user_id: continue try: await ws.send_text(json.dumps(message)) except Exception: dead.append(uid) for uid in dead: self.disconnect(session_id, uid) async def send_to_user(self, session_id: str, user_id: str, message: dict) -> None: """Send a message to one specific user.""" ws = self._rooms.get(session_id, {}).get(user_id) if ws: try: await ws.send_text(json.dumps(message)) except Exception: self.disconnect(session_id, user_id) def peer_ids(self, session_id: str, exclude_user_id: str) -> list[str]: return [uid for uid in self._rooms.get(session_id, {}) if uid != exclude_user_id] async def send_state_snapshot(self, ws: WebSocket, session_id: str, db: AsyncSession) -> None: from sqlalchemy import select from app.modules.sessions.models import Session result = await db.execute(select(Session).where(Session.id == session_id)) session = result.scalar_one_or_none() if session: snapshot = { "type": "room_state", "session_status": session.status, "enrolled_count": session.enrolled_count, } await ws.send_text(json.dumps(snapshot)) room_manager = RoomConnectionManager() @router.websocket("/api/ws/sessions/{session_id}/room") async def session_room_ws( session_id: str, websocket: WebSocket, db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db), ): """ WebSocket endpoint for live session room. Authenticate via ?token=. """ # Authenticate try: user = await get_ws_user(websocket, db) except Exception: return # get_ws_user closes the socket # Verify user has access (confirmed booking, or admin/instructor) if user.role not in ("admin", "instructor"): from sqlalchemy import select, and_ from app.modules.bookings.models import Booking, BookingStatus result = await db.execute( select(Booking).where( and_( Booking.session_id == session_id, Booking.learner_id == user.id, Booking.status == BookingStatus.confirmed, ) ) ) if not result.scalar_one_or_none(): await websocket.close(code=4003) return user_id = str(user.id) await room_manager.connect(session_id, user_id, websocket) # Send current room state on join await room_manager.send_state_snapshot(websocket, session_id, db) # Notify others that a peer joined await room_manager.broadcast(session_id, { "type": "peer_joined", "user_id": user_id, "role": user.role, }, exclude_user_id=user_id) # Tell the joining user which peers are already in the room await websocket.send_text(json.dumps({ "type": "room_peers", "peers": [{"user_id": uid} for uid in room_manager.peer_ids(session_id, user_id)], })) try: while True: raw = await websocket.receive_text() try: msg = json.loads(raw) msg_type = msg.get("type", "") if msg_type in ("webrtc_offer", "webrtc_answer", "webrtc_ice"): msg["from_user_id"] = user_id target = msg.get("target_user_id") if target: await room_manager.send_to_user(session_id, target, msg) else: await room_manager.broadcast(session_id, msg, exclude_user_id=user_id) except Exception: pass except WebSocketDisconnect: room_manager.disconnect(session_id, user_id) await room_manager.broadcast(session_id, { "type": "peer_left", "user_id": user_id, "role": user.role, }) ``` ## 7. backend/docker-entrypoint.sh ```bash #!/bin/bash set -e echo "==> Waiting for PostgreSQL..." until pg_isready -h "${DB_HOST:-db}" -p "${DB_PORT:-5432}" -U "${DB_USER:-meridian}"; Exception: self.disconnect(session_id, user_id) def peer_ids(self, session_id: str, exclude_user_id: str) -> list[str]: return [uid for uid in self._rooms.get(session_id, {}) if uid != exclude_user_id] async def send_state_snapshot(self, ws: WebSocket, session_id: str, db: AsyncSession) -> None: from sqlalchemy import select from app.modules.sessions.models import Session result = await db.execute(select(Session).where(Session.id == session_id)) session = result.scalar_one_or_none() if session: snapshot = { "type": "room_state", "session_status": session.status, "enrolled_count": session.enrolled_count, } await ws.send_text(json.dumps(snapshot)) room_manager = RoomConnectionManager() @router.websocket("/api/ws/sessions/{session_id}/room") async def session_room_ws( session_id: str, websocket: WebSocket, db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db), ): """ WebSocket endpoint for live session room. Authenticate via ?token=. """ # Authenticate try: user = await get_ws_user(websocket, db) except Exception: return # get_ws_user closes the socket # Verify user has access (confirmed booking, or admin/instructor) if user.role not in ("admin", "instructor"): from sqlalchemy import select, and_ from app.modules.bookings.models import Booking, BookingStatus result = await db.execute( select(Booking).where( and_( Booking.session_id == session_id, Booking.learner_id == user.id, Booking.status == BookingStatus.confirmed, ) ) ) if not result.scalar_one_or_none(): await websocket.close(code=4003) return user_id = str(user.id) await room_manager.connect(session_id, user_id, websocket) # Send current room state on join await room_manager.send_state_snapshot(websocket, session_id, db) # Notify others that a peer joined await room_manager.broadcast(session_id, { "type": "peer_joined", "user_id": user_id, "role": user.role, }, exclude_user_id=user_id) # Tell the joining user which peers are already in the room await websocket.send_text(json.dumps({ "type": "room_peers", "peers": [{"user_id": uid} for uid in room_manager.peer_ids(session_id, user_id)], })) try: while True: raw = await websocket.receive_text() try: msg = json.loads(raw) msg_type = msg.get("type", "") if msg_type in ("webrtc_offer", "webrtc_answer", "webrtc_ice"): msg["from_user_id"] = user_id target = msg.get("target_user_id") if target: await room_manager.send_to_user(session_id, target, msg) else: await room_manager.broadcast(session_id, msg, exclude_user_id=user_id) except Exception: pass except WebSocketDisconnect: room_manager.disconnect(session_id, user_id) await room_manager.broadcast(session_id, { "type": "peer_left", "user_id": user_id, "role": user.role, }) ``` --- ## 7. 16	def _build_response(user: User, admin: bool = False) -> UserResponse | UserDetailResponse:
17	    email_raw = decrypt_field(user.email_encrypted) if user.email_encrypted else None
18	    phone_raw = decrypt_field(user.phone_encrypted) if user.phone_encrypted else None
19	
20	    data = {
21	        "id": user.id,
22	        "username": user.username,
23	        "role": user.role,
24	        "email": mask_email(email_raw),
25	        "phone": mask_phone(phone_raw),
26	        "is_active": user.is_active,
27	        "last_login": user.last_login,
28	        "created_at": user.created_at,
29	    }
30	    if admin:
31	        return UserDetailResponse(**data, email_unmasked=email_raw, phone_unmasked=phone_raw)
32	    return UserResponse(**data)

35	class UserService:
36	    def __init__(self, db: AsyncSession) -> None:
37	        self._repo = UserRepository(db)
38	
39	    async def create(self, payload: UserCreate, actor_id: str | None = None) -> UserResponse:
40	        existing = await self._repo.get_by_username(payload.username)
41	        if existing:
42	            raise ConflictError(f"Username '{payload.username}' already exists.")
43	
44	        user = User(
45	            username=payload.username,
46	            password_hash=hash_password(payload.password),
47	            role=payload.role,
48	            email_encrypted=encrypt_field(payload.email) if payload.email else None,
49	            phone_encrypted=encrypt_field(payload.phone) if payload.phone else None,
50	        )
51	        user = await self._repo.create(user)
52	
53	        if payload.role == UserRole.instructor:
54	            self._repo._db.add(Instructor(user_id=user.id))
55	
56	        await log_audit(self._repo._db, actor_id or str(user.id), "user", str(user.id), "create")
57	        return _build_response(user, admin=True)

60	        user = await self._repo.get_by_id(user_id)
61	        if not user:
62	            raise NotFoundError("User")

64

66	        users, total = await self._repo.list_users(page, page_size, role, is_active)
67	        items = [_build_response(u, admin=True) for u in users]
68	        return items, total
69	
70	    async def update(self, user_id: str, payload: UserUpdate, actor_id: str | None = None) -> UserResponse:
71	        user = await self._repo.get_by_id(user_id)
72	        if not user:
73	            raise NotFoundError("User")
74	
75	        data: dict = {}
76	        if payload.email is not None:
77	            data["email_encrypted"] = encrypt_field(payload.email)
78	        if payload.phone is not None:
79	            data["phone_encrypted"] = encrypt_field(payload.phone)
80	        if payload.is_active is not None:
81	            data["is_active"] = payload.is_active
82	        if payload.role is not None:
83	            data["role"] = payload.role
84	
85	        user = await self._repo.update(user, data)
86	
87	        if data.get("role") == UserRole.instructor:
88	            from sqlalchemy import select

91	            )

93	                self._repo._db.add(Instructor(user_id=user.id))
94	
95	        await log_audit(self._repo._db, actor_id or user_id, "user", user_id, "update",
96	                        new_value={k: str(v) for k, v in data.items() if k not in ("email_encrypted", "phone_encrypted")})
97	        return _build_response(user, admin=True)
98	
99	    async def delete(self, user_id: str, actor_id: str | None = None) -> None:
100	        user = await self._repo.get_by_id(user_id)
101	        if not user:
102	            raise NotFoundError("User")
103	        await self._repo.soft_delete(user)
104	        await log_audit(self._repo._db, actor_id or user_id, "user", user_id, "delete")

1	"""WebSocket live-room manager and endpoint."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import json
6	import logging
7	from collections import defaultdict
8	
9	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, WebSocket, WebSocketDisconnect
10	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
11	
12	from app.core.database import get_db
13	from app.core.deps import get_ws_user
14	
15	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
16	
17	router = APIRouter(tags=["Live Room WebSocket"])
18	
19	
20	class RoomConnectionManager:
21	    """In-process connection manager keyed by session_id, tracking user identity."""
22	
23	    def __init__(self) -> None:
24	        # session_id → {user_id: websocket}
25	        self._rooms: dict[str, dict[str, WebSocket]] = defaultdict(dict)
26	
27	    async def connect(self, session_id: str, user_id: str, ws: WebSocket) -> None:
28	        await ws.accept()
29	        self._rooms[session_id][user_id] = ws
30	        logger.debug("WS connected: session=%s user=%s total=%d", session_id, user_id, len(self._rooms[session_id]))
31	
32	    def disconnect(self, session_id: str, user_id: str) -> None:
33	        self._rooms[session_id].pop(user_id, None)
34	        if not self._rooms[session_id]:
35	            self._rooms.pop(session_id, None)
36	
37	    async def broadcast(self, session_id: str, message: dict, exclude_user_id: str | None = None) -> None:
38	        """Broadcast JSON to all clients in a room, optionally excluding one sender."""
39	        dead: list[str] = []
40	        for uid, ws in list(self._rooms.get(session_id, {}).items()):
41	            if uid == exclude_user_id:
42	                continue
43	            try:
44	                await ws.send_text(json.dumps(message))
45	            except Exception:
46	                dead.append(uid)
47	        for uid in dead:
48	            self.disconnect(session_id, uid)
49	
50	    async def send_to_user(self, session_id: str, user_id: str, message: dict) -> None:
51	        """Send a message to one specific user."""
52	        ws = self._rooms.get(session_id, {}).get(user_id)
53	        if ws:
54	            try:
55	                await ws.send_text(json.dumps(message))
56	            except Exception:
57	                self.disconnect(session_id, user_id)
58	
59	    def peer_ids(self, session_id: str, exclude_user_id: str) -> list[str]:
60	        return [uid for uid in self._rooms.get(session_id, {}) if uid != exclude_user_id]
61	
62	    async def send_state_snapshot(self, ws: WebSocket, session_id: str, db: AsyncSession) -> None:
63	        from sqlalchemy import select
64	        from app.modules.sessions.models import Session
65	
66	        result = await db.execute(select(Session).where(Session.id == session_id))
67	        session = result.scalar_one_or_none()
68	        if session:
69	            snapshot = {
70	                "type": "room_state",
71	                "session_status": session.status,
72	                "enrolled_count": session.enrolled_count,
73	            }
74	            await ws.send_text(json.dumps(snapshot))
75	
76	
77	room_manager = RoomConnectionManager()
78	
79	
80	@router.websocket("/api/ws/sessions/{session_id}/room")
81	async def session_room_ws(
82	    session_id: str,
83	    websocket: WebSocket,
84	    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
85	):
86	    """
87	    WebSocket endpoint for live session room.
88	    Authenticate via ?token=<access_jwt>.
89	    """
90	    # Authenticate
91	    try:
92	        user = await get_ws_user(websocket, db)
93	    except Exception:
94	        return  # get_ws_user closes the socket
95	
96	    # Verify user has access (confirmed booking, or admin/instructor)
97	    if user.role not in ("admin", "instructor"):
98	        from sqlalchemy import select, and_
99	        from app.modules.bookings.models import Booking, BookingStatus
100	        result = await db.execute(
101	            select(Booking).where(
102	                and_(
103	                    Booking.session_id == session_id,
104	                    Booking.learner_id == user.id,
105	                    Booking.status == BookingStatus.confirmed,
106	                )
107	            )
108	        )
109	        if not result.scalar_one_or_none():
110	            await websocket.close(code=4003)
111	            return
112	
113	    user_id = str(user.id)
114	    await room_manager.connect(session_id, user_id, websocket)
115	    # Send current room state on join
116	    await room_manager.send_state_snapshot(websocket, session_id, db)
117	    # Notify others that a peer joined
118	    await room_manager.broadcast(session_id, {
119	        "type": "peer_joined", "user_id": user_id, "role": user.role,
120	    }, exclude_user_id=user_id)
121	    # Tell the joining user which peers are already in the room
122	    await websocket.send_text(json.dumps({
123	        "type": "room_peers",
124	        "peers": [{"user_id": uid} for uid in room_manager.peer_ids(session_id, user_id)],
125	    }))
126	
127	    try:
128	        while True:
129	            raw = await websocket.receive_text()
130	            try:
131	                msg = json.loads(raw)
132	                msg_type = msg.get("type", "")
133	                if msg_type in ("webrtc_offer", "webrtc_answer", "webrtc_ice"):
134	                    msg["from_user_id"] = user_id
135	                    target = msg.get("target_user_id")
136	                    if target:
137	                        await room_manager.send_to_user(session_id, target, msg)
138	                    else:
139	                        await room_manager.broadcast(session_id, msg, exclude_user_id=user_id)
140	            except Exception:
141	                pass
142	    except WebSocketDisconnect:
143	        room_manager.disconnect(session_id, user_id)
144	        await room_manager.broadcast(session_id, {
145	            "type": "peer_left", "user_id": user_id, "role": user.role,
146	        })
147

1	#!/bin/bash
2	set -e
3	
4	echo "==> Waiting for PostgreSQL..."
5	until pg_isready -h "${DB_HOST:-db}" -p "${DB_PORT:-5432}" -U "${DB_USER:-meridian}"; do
6	  sleep 1
7	done
8	echo "==> PostgreSQL is ready."
9	
10	echo "==> Running Alembic migrations..."
11	cd /app
12	alembic -c alembic/alembic.ini upgrade head
13	
14	echo "==> Seeding default admin user if needed..."
15	python -c "
16	import asyncio
17	from app.core.database import AsyncSessionLocal
18	from app.core.security import hash_password
19	from app.modules.users.models import User, UserRole
20	from sqlalchemy import select
21	
22	async def seed():
23	    async with AsyncSessionLocal() as db:
24	        result = await db.execute(select(User).where(User.username == 'admin'))
25	        if not result.scalar_one_or_none():
26	            admin = User(
27	                username='admin',
28	                password_hash=hash_password('Admin@Meridian1'),
29	                role=UserRole.admin,
30	                is_active=True,
31	            )
32	            db.add(admin)
33	            await db.commit()
34	            print('Default admin user created.')
35	        else:
36	            print('Admin user already exists.')
37	
38	asyncio.run(seed())
39	"
40	
41	echo "==> Starting Uvicorn..."
42	exec uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --workers 4 --log-level info
43

1	"""FastAPI dependency injection: current user, role guards."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from fastapi import Depends, WebSocket
6	from fastapi.security import OAuth2PasswordBearer
7	from jose import JWTError
8	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
9	
10	from app.core.database import get_db
11	from app.core.exceptions import ForbiddenError, UnauthorizedError
12	from app.core.security import decode_token, is_inactive, is_token_blocklisted, touch_last_seen
13	
14	oauth2_scheme = OAuth2PasswordBearer(tokenUrl="/api/auth/login")
15	
16	
17	async def get_current_user(
18	    token: str = Depends(oauth2_scheme),
19	    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
20	):
21	    from app.modules.users.repository import UserRepository
22	
23	    try:
24	        payload = decode_token(token)
25	    except JWTError:
26	        raise UnauthorizedError("Invalid or expired token.")
27	
28	    if payload.get("type") != "access":
29	        raise UnauthorizedError("Invalid token type.")
30	
31	    if await is_token_blocklisted(token):
32	        raise UnauthorizedError("Token has been revoked.")
33	
34	    user_id: str = payload.get("sub")
35	    if not user_id:
36	        raise UnauthorizedError()
37	
38	    if await is_inactive(user_id):
39	        raise UnauthorizedError("Session expired due to inactivity.")
40	
41	    repo = UserRepository(db)
42	    user = await repo.get_by_id(user_id)
43	    if not user or not user.is_active:
44	        raise UnauthorizedError("User not found or inactive.")
45	
46	    await touch_last_seen(user_id)
47	    return user
48	
49	
50	def require_roles(*roles: str):
51	    """Returns a dependency that enforces role membership."""
52	
53	    async def _check(current_user=Depends(get_current_user)):
54	        if current_user.role not in roles:
55	            raise ForbiddenError()
56	        return current_user
57	
58	    return _check
59	
60	
61	async def get_ws_user(websocket: WebSocket, db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)):
62	    """Authenticate WebSocket connections via ?token= query param."""
63	    from app.modules.users.repository import UserRepository
64	
65	    token = websocket.query_params.get("token")
66	    if not token:
67	        await websocket.close(code=4001)
68	        raise UnauthorizedError("Missing token.")
69	
70	    try:
71	        payload = decode_token(token)
72	    except JWTError:
73	        await websocket.close(code=4001)
74	        raise UnauthorizedError("Invalid token.")
75	
76	    if payload.get("type") != "access":
77	        await websocket.close(code=4001)
78	        raise UnauthorizedError()
79	
80	    if await is_token_blocklisted(token):
81	        await websocket.close(code=4001)
82	        raise UnauthorizedError("Token has been revoked.")
83	
84	    user_id: str = payload.get("sub")
85	
86	    if await is_inactive(user_id):
87	        await websocket.close(code=4001)
88	        raise UnauthorizedError("Session expired due to inactivity.")
89	
90	    repo = UserRepository(db)
91	    user = await repo.get_by_id(user_id)
92	    if not user or not user.is_active:
93	        await websocket.close(code=4001)
94	        raise UnauthorizedError()
95	
96	    await touch_last_seen(user_id)
97	    return user

1	import uuid

3	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, Query, Request, UploadFile, File, Form
4	from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse
5	from jose import JWTError
6	from sqlalchemy import and_, select
7	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
8	
9	from app.core.database import get_db
10	from app.core.deps import get_current_user, require_roles
11	from app.core.exceptions import NotFoundError, UnauthorizedError
12	from app.core.security import decode_token, is_token_blocklisted
13	from app.core.storage import get_object_chunks, stat_object
14	from app.modules.replays.models import RecordingUploadStatus, SessionRecording
15	from app.modules.replays.schemas import (
16	    ReplayAccessRuleCreate, ReplayAccessRuleResponse, ReplayStats,
17	    ReplayViewCreate, RecordingResponse,
18	)
19	from app.modules.replays.service import ReplayService
20	
21	router = APIRouter(tags=["Replays"])
22	
23	
24	async def _resolve_stream_user(token: str, db: AsyncSession):
25	    """Resolve a user from a ?token= query param (used for video streaming)."""
26	    from app.modules.users.repository import UserRepository
27	    try:
28	        payload = decode_token(token)
29	    except JWTError:
30	        raise UnauthorizedError("Invalid token.")
31	    if payload.get("type") != "access":
32	        raise UnauthorizedError("Invalid token type.")
33	    if await is_token_blocklisted(token):
34	        raise UnauthorizedError("Token has been revoked.")
35	    user_id: str = payload.get("sub")
36	    if not user_id:
37	        raise UnauthorizedError()
38	    user = await UserRepository(db).get_by_id(user_id)
39	    if not user or not user.is_active:
40	        raise UnauthorizedError("User not found or inactive.")
41	    return user
42	
43	
44	@router.get("/sessions/{session_id}/recordings", response_model=list[RecordingResponse])
45	async def list_recordings(
46	    session_id: uuid.UUID,
47	    current_user=Depends(get_current_user),
48	    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
49	):
50	    """List all ready recordings for a session (newest first)."""
51	    return await ReplayService(db).get_all_recordings(session_id, current_user.id, current_user.role)
52	
53	
54	@router.get("/sessions/{session_id}/recordings/{recording_id}/stream")
55	async def stream_recording(
56	    session_id: uuid.UUID,
57	    recording_id: uuid.UUID,
58	    request: Request,
59	    token: str = Query(...),
60	    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
61	):
62	    """
63	    Stream a recording directly from MinIO through the backend.
64	    Accepts the JWT as ?token= so it can be used as a plain <video src=>.
65	    Supports HTTP Range requests for video seeking.
66	    """
67	    user = await _resolve_stream_user(token, db)
68	    await ReplayService(db)._check_access(session_id, user.id, user.role)
69	
70	    result = await db.execute(
71	        select(SessionRecording).where(
72	            and_(
73	                SessionRecording.id == recording_id,
74	                SessionRecording.session_id == session_id,
75	                SessionRecording.upload_status == RecordingUploadStatus.ready,
76	            )
77	        )
78	    )
79	    recording = result.scalar_one_or_none()
80	    if not recording:
81	        raise NotFoundError("Recording")
82	
83	    stat = stat_object(recording.bucket_name, recording.object_storage_key)
84	    total_size = stat.size
85	    content_type = recording.mime_type or stat.content_type or "video/webm"
86	
87	    range_header = request.headers.get("Range")
88	    if range_header:
89	        # Parse "bytes=start-end"
90	        range_val = range_header.strip().replace("bytes=", "")
91	        start_str, _, end_str = range_val.partition("-")
92	        start = int(start_str) if start_str else 0
93	        end = int(end_str) if end_str else total_size - 1
94	        end = min(end, total_size - 1)
95	        length = end - start + 1
96	        return StreamingResponse(
97	            get_object_chunks(recording.bucket_name, recording.object_storage_key, offset=start, length=length),
98	            status_code=206,
99	            media_type=content_type,
100	            headers={
101	                "Content-Range": f"bytes {start}-{end}/{total_size}",
102	                "Accept-Ranges": "bytes",
103	                "Content-Length": str(length),
104	            },
105	        )
106	
107	    return StreamingResponse(
108	        get_object_chunks(recording.bucket_name, recording.object_storage_key),
109	        status_code=200,
110	        media_type=content_type,
111	        headers={
112	            "Accept-Ranges": "bytes",
113	            "Content-Length": str(total_size),
114	        },
115	    )
116	
117

24	async def _resolve_stream_user(token: str, db: AsyncSession):
25	    """Resolve a user from a ?token= query param (used for video streaming)."""
26	    from app.modules.users.repository import UserRepository
27	    try:
28	        payload = decode_token(token)
29	    except JWTError:
30	        raise UnauthorizedError("Invalid token.")
31	    if payload.get("type") != "access":
32	        raise UnauthorizedError("Invalid token type.")
33	    if await is_token_blocklisted(token):
34	        raise UnauthorizedError("Token has been revoked.")
35	    user_id: str = payload.get("sub")
36	    if not user_id:
37	        raise UnauthorizedError()
38	    user = await UserRepository(db).get_by_id(user_id)
39	    if not user or not user.is_active:
40	        raise UnauthorizedError("User not found or inactive.")
41	    return user
42

52

54	@router.get("/sessions/{session_id}/recordings/{recording_id}/stream")
55	async def stream_recording(
56	    session_id: uuid.UUID,
57	    recording_id: uuid.UUID,
58	    request: Request,
59	    token: str = Query(...),
60	    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
61	):
62	    """
63	    Stream a recording directly from MinIO through the backend.
64	    Accepts the JWT as ?token= so it can be used as a plain <video src=>.
65	    Supports HTTP Range requests for video seeking.
66	    """
67	    user = await _resolve_stream_user(token, db)
68	    await ReplayService(db)._check_access(session_id, user.id, user.role)
69	
70	    result = await db.execute(
71	        select(SessionRecording).where(
72	            and_(
73	                SessionRecording.id == recording_id,
74	                SessionRecording.session_id == session_id,
75	                SessionRecording.upload_status == RecordingUploadStatus.ready,
76	            )
77	        )
78	    )
79	    recording = result.scalar_one_or_none()
80	    if not recording:
81	        raise NotFoundError("Recording")
82	
83	    stat = stat_object(recording.bucket_name, recording.object_storage_key)
84	    total_size = stat.size
85	    content_type = recording.mime_type or stat.content_type or "video/webm"
86	
87	    range_header = request.headers.get("Range")
88	    if range_header:
89	        # Parse "bytes=start-end"
90	        range_val = range_header.strip().replace("bytes=", "")
91	        start_str, _, end_str = range_val.partition("-")
92	        start = int(start_str) if start_str else 0
93	        end = int(end_str) if end_str else total_size - 1
94	        end = min(end, total_size - 1)
95	        length = end - start + 1
96	        return StreamingResponse(
97	            get_object_chunks(recording.bucket_name, recording.object_storage_key, offset=start, length=length),
98	            status_code=206,
99	            media_type=content_type,
100	            headers={
101	                "Content-Range": f"bytes {start}-{end}/{total_size}",
102	                "Accept-Ranges": "bytes",
103	                "Content-Length": str(length),
104	            },
105	        )
106	
107	    return StreamingResponse(
108	        get_object_chunks(recording.bucket_name, recording.object_storage_key),
109	        status_code=200,
110	        media_type=content_type,
111	        headers={
112	            "Accept-Ranges": "bytes",
113	            "Content-Length": str(total_size),
114	        },
115	    )
116

3	import uuid
4	from datetime import UTC, datetime
5	
6	from sqlalchemy import and_, func, select, desc
7	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
8	
9	from app.core.config import settings
10	from app.core.exceptions import ForbiddenError, GoneError, NotFoundError
11	from app.core.storage import get_presigned_download_url, get_presigned_upload_url
12	from app.modules.bookings.models import Booking, BookingStatus
13	from app.modules.replays.models import (
14	    RecordingUploadStatus, ReplayAccessRule, ReplayRuleType,
15	    ReplayView, SessionRecording,
16	)
17	from app.modules.replays.schemas import (
18	    ReplayAccessRuleCreate, ReplayAccessRuleResponse, ReplayStats,
19	    ReplayViewCreate, RecordingResponse,
20	)
21	from app.modules.sessions.repository import SessionRepository
22	
23	
24	class ReplayService:
25	    def __init__(self, db: AsyncSession) -> None:
26	        self._db = db
27	        self._session_repo = SessionRepository(db)
28	
29	    async def _check_access(self, session_id: uuid.UUID, user_id: uuid.UUID, user_role: str) -> None:
30	        """Raise ForbiddenError if user cannot access the replay."""
31	        if user_role in ("admin", "instructor"):
32	            return
33	
34	        result = await self._db.execute(
35	            select(ReplayAccessRule).where(
36	                and_(ReplayAccessRule.session_id == session_id, ReplayAccessRule.is_active == True)
37	            )
38	        )
39	        rule = result.scalar_one_or_none()
40	
41	        now = datetime.now(UTC)
42	        if rule:
43	            if rule.available_from and now < rule.available_from:
44	                raise ForbiddenError("Replay is not yet available.")
45	            if rule.available_until and now > rule.available_until:
46	                raise GoneError("Replay access has expired.")
47	            if rule.rule_type == ReplayRuleType.public:
48	                return
49	
50	        # Default (no rule) and enrolled_only rule: require a confirmed booking
51	        booking_result = await self._db.execute(
52	            select(Booking).where(
53	                and_(
54	                    Booking.session_id == session_id,
55	                    Booking.learner_id == user_id,
56	                    Booking.status == BookingStatus.confirmed,
57	                )
58	            )
59	        )
60	        if not booking_result.scalar_one_or_none():
61	            raise ForbiddenError("Only enrolled learners can access this replay.")
62	
63	    async def get_replay(self, session_id: uuid.UUID, user_id: uuid.UUID, user_role: str) -> RecordingResponse:
64	        await self._check_access(session_id, user_id, user_role)
65	        result = await self._db.execute(
66	            select(SessionRecording)
67	            .where(
68	                and_(
69	                    SessionRecording.session_id == session_id,
70	                    SessionRecording.upload_status == RecordingUploadStatus.ready,
71	                )
72	            )
73	            .order_by(desc(SessionRecording.created_at))
74	            .limit(1)
75	        )
76	        recording = result.scalar_one_or_none()
77	        if not recording:
78	            raise NotFoundError("Recording")
79	
80	        resp = RecordingResponse.model_validate(recording)
81	        return resp
82	
83	    async def get_all_recordings(self, session_id: uuid.UUID, user_id: uuid.UUID, user_role: str) -> list[RecordingResponse]:
84	        await self._check_access(session_id, user_id, user_role)
85	        result = await self._db.execute(
86	            select(SessionRecording)
87	            .where(
88	                and_(
89	                    SessionRecording.session_id == session_id,
90	                    SessionRecording.upload_status == RecordingUploadStatus.ready,
91	                )
92	            )
93	            .order_by(desc(SessionRecording.created_at))
94	        )
95	        recordings = result.scalars().all()
96	        return [RecordingResponse.model_validate(r) for r in recordings]
97	
98	    async def initiate_upload(self, session_id: uuid.UUID) -> dict:
99	        """Returns a presigned PUT URL for direct upload to MinIO."""
100	        session = await self._session_repo.get(session_id)
101	        if not session:
102	            raise NotFoundError("Session")
103	
104	        object_key = f"sessions/{session_id}/recording.mp4"
105	        bucket = settings.MINIO_BUCKET_RECORDINGS
106	
107	        # Create or update recording metadata row
108	        result = await self._db.execute(
109	            select(SessionRecording).where(SessionRecording.session_id == session_id)
110	        )
111	        recording = result.scalar_one_or_none()
112	        if not recording:
113	            recording = SessionRecording(
114	                session_id=session_id,
115	                object_storage_key=object_key,
116	                bucket_name=bucket,
117	                upload_status=RecordingUploadStatus.pending,
118	            )
119	            self._db.add(recording)
120	        else:
121	            recording.object_storage_key = object_key
122	            recording.upload_status = RecordingUploadStatus.pending
123	        await self._db.flush()
124	
125	        presigned_url = get_presigned_upload_url(bucket, object_key)
126	        return {"presigned_url": presigned_url, "object_key": object_key, "bucket": bucket}
127	
128	    async def confirm_upload(self, session_id: uuid.UUID, file_size_bytes: int | None, duration_seconds: int | None) -> RecordingResponse:
129	        result = await self._db.execute(
130	            select(SessionRecording).where(SessionRecording.session_id == session_id)
131	        )
132	        recording = result.scalar_one_or_none()
133	        if not recording:
134	            raise NotFoundError("Recording")
135	        recording.upload_status = RecordingUploadStatus.ready
136	        if file_size_bytes:
137	            recording.file_size_bytes = file_size_bytes
138	        if duration_seconds:
139	            recording.duration_seconds = duration_seconds
140	        await self._db.flush()
141	        return RecordingResponse.model_validate(recording)
142	
143	    async def store_recording_direct(
144	        self, session_id: uuid.UUID, data: bytes, file_size_bytes: int, duration_seconds: int, content_type: str
145	    ) -> RecordingResponse:
146	        """Upload recording bytes directly to MinIO (used when presigned URLs are unreachable from browser)."""
147	        from app.core.storage import upload_object
148	
149	        session = await self._session_repo.get(session_id)
150	        if not session:
151	            raise NotFoundError("Session")
152	
153	        recording_id = uuid.uuid4()
154	        # Derive extension from content_type (webm or mp4)
155	        ext = "webm" if "webm" in content_type else "mp4"
156	        object_key = f"sessions/{session_id}/{recording_id}.{ext}"
157	        bucket = settings.MINIO_BUCKET_RECORDINGS
158	
159	        upload_object(bucket, object_key, data, content_type)
160	
161	        recording = SessionRecording(
162	            id=recording_id,
163	            session_id=session_id,
164	            object_storage_key=object_key,
165	            bucket_name=bucket,
166	            mime_type=content_type,
167	            upload_status=RecordingUploadStatus.ready,
168	            file_size_bytes=file_size_bytes,
169	            duration_seconds=duration_seconds,
170	        )
171	        self._db.add(recording)
172	        await self._db.flush()
173	        return RecordingResponse.model_validate(recording)
174	
175	    async def set_access_rule(self, session_id: uuid.UUID, payload: ReplayAccessRuleCreate) -> ReplayAccessRuleResponse:
176	        result = await self._db.execute(
177	            select(ReplayAccessRule).where(ReplayAccessRule.session_id == session_id)
178	        )
179	        rule = result.scalar_one_or_none()
180	        if rule:
181	            rule.rule_type = payload.rule_type
182	            rule.available_from = payload.available_from
183	            rule.available_until = payload.available_until
184	            rule.is_active = True
185	        else:
186	            rule = ReplayAccessRule(session_id=session_id, **payload.model_dump())
187	            self._db.add(rule)
188	        await self._db.flush()
189	        return ReplayAccessRuleResponse.model_validate(rule)
190	
191	    async def record_view(self, session_id: uuid.UUID, learner_id: uuid.UUID, payload: ReplayViewCreate) -> None:
192	        view = ReplayView(
193	            session_id=session_id,
194	            learner_id=learner_id,
195	            watched_seconds=payload.watched_seconds,
196	            completed=payload.completed,
197	        )
198	        self._db.add(view)
199	        await self._db.flush()
200	
201	    async def get_stats(self, session_id: uuid.UUID) -> ReplayStats:
202	        total_result = await self._db.execute(
203	            select(func.count()).where(ReplayView.session_id == session_id)
204	        )
205	        total = total_result.scalar_one()
206	
207	        unique_result = await self._db.execute(
208	            select(func.count(ReplayView.learner_id.distinct())).where(ReplayView.session_id == session_id)
209	        )
210	        unique = unique_result.scalar_one()
211	
212	        completed_result = await self._db.execute(
213	            select(func.count()).where(
214	                and_(ReplayView.session_id == session_id, ReplayView.completed == True)
215	            )
216	        )
217	        completed = completed_result.scalar_one()
218	        rate = (completed / total * 100) if total else 0.0
219	
220	        return ReplayStats(
221	            session_id=session_id,
222	            total_views=total,
223	            unique_viewers=unique,
224	            completion_rate_pct=round(rate, 1),
225	        )
226

187	            ))

189	        elapsed = (time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000

194	            facets = await self._compute_facets(filters)

1	"""
2	Best-offer promotion engine.
3	
4	Default behavior: MUTUAL EXCLUSION (per spec).
5	- null stack_group  → singleton exclusive group; only best one from all null-group promos applies
6	- same stack_group  → mutually exclusive within group (best one per group)
7	- different non-null groups → stackable across groups
8	- is_exclusive=True → cannot combine with anything, even across groups
9	"""
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import itertools
13	from dataclasses import dataclass
14	from datetime import UTC, datetime
15	from typing import Any
16	
17	from app.modules.promotions.models import Promotion, PromotionType
18	
19	
20	@dataclass
21	class AppliedPromotion:
22	    promotion: Promotion
23	    discount_amount: float
24	    explanation: str
25	
26	
27	def _calc_discount(promo: Promotion, subtotal: float, course_ids: set[str]) -> float:
28	    """Calculate the discount amount a single promotion provides on the subtotal."""
29	    # Check minimum order amount
30	    if promo.min_order_amount and subtotal < float(promo.min_order_amount):
31	        return 0.0
32	
33	    # Check course scope
34	    applies = promo.applies_to or {}
35	    if not applies.get("all"):
36	        allowed_course_ids = set(applies.get("course_ids", []))
37	        if not allowed_course_ids.intersection(course_ids):
38	            return 0.0
39	
40	    if promo.type == PromotionType.pct_off:
41	        return round(subtotal * float(promo.value) / 100, 2)
42	    elif promo.type == PromotionType.fixed_off:
43	        return round(min(float(promo.value), subtotal), 2)
44	    elif promo.type == PromotionType.bogo:
45	        # Buy one get one: 50% off the subtotal (simplification for offline context)
46	        return round(subtotal * 0.5, 2)
47	    return 0.0
48	
49	
50	def _build_explanation(promo: Promotion, discount: float) -> str:
51	    if promo.type == PromotionType.pct_off:
52	        return f"{promo.name}: {promo.value}% off → -${discount:.2f}"
53	    elif promo.type == PromotionType.fixed_off:
54	        return f"{promo.name}: ${promo.value:.2f} off → -${discount:.2f}"
55	    elif promo.type == PromotionType.bogo:
56	        return f"{promo.name}: BOGO 50% off → -${discount:.2f}"
57	    return f"{promo.name}: -${discount:.2f}"
58	
59	
60	def _group_promotions(promotions: list[Promotion]) -> dict[str | None, list[Promotion]]:
61	    """Group promotions by stack_group. None-keyed = singleton/exclusive group."""
62	    groups: dict[str | None, list[Promotion]] = {}
63	    for p in promotions:
64	        groups.setdefault(p.stack_group, []).append(p)
65	    return groups
66	
67	
68	def compute_best_offer(
69	    promotions: list[Promotion],
70	    subtotal: float,
71	    course_ids: set[str],
72	) -> list[AppliedPromotion]:
73	    """
74	    Returns the combination of promotions that maximises discount.
75	
76	    Stack rules (mutual exclusion by default):
77	    - is_exclusive promotions: tested alone only; never combined.
78	    - null stack_group: only the single best promo from the entire null pool applies.
79	    - same non-null stack_group: only the best promo from that group.
80	    - different non-null groups: one from each group may stack.
81	    """
82	    now = datetime.now(UTC)
83	    valid = [
84	        p for p in promotions
85	        if p.is_active and p.valid_from <= now <= p.valid_until
86	    ]
87	
88	    if not valid:
89	        return []
90	
91	    # --- Step 1: separate exclusive promos ---
92	    exclusive = [p for p in valid if p.is_exclusive]
93	    non_exclusive = [p for p in valid if not p.is_exclusive]
94	
95	    def _apply_single(promo: Promotion) -> list[AppliedPromotion]:
96	        d = _calc_discount(promo, subtotal, course_ids)
97	        if d <= 0:
98	            return []
99	        return [AppliedPromotion(promo, d, _build_explanation(promo, d))]
100	
101	    best: list[AppliedPromotion] = []
102	    best_total: float = 0.0
103	
104	    # --- Step 2: test each exclusive promo alone ---
105	    for p in exclusive:
106	        candidate = _apply_single(p)
107	        total = sum(a.discount_amount for a in candidate)
108	        if total > best_total:
109	            best_total = total
110	            best = candidate
111	
112	    # --- Step 3: group non-exclusive promos ---
113	    groups = _group_promotions(non_exclusive)
114	
115	    # Separate null-group (mutually exclusive by default) from named groups
116	    null_group = groups.pop(None, [])
117	    named_groups = groups  # dict[str, list[Promotion]]
118	
119	    # From the null group, pick the single best promo (mutual exclusion default)
120	    best_null: Promotion | None = None
121	    best_null_discount: float = 0.0
122	    for p in null_group:
123	        d = _calc_discount(p, subtotal, course_ids)
124	        if d > best_null_discount:
125	            best_null_discount = d
126	            best_null = p
127	
128	    # For each named group, pick the best promo from that group
129	    best_per_group: dict[str, tuple[Promotion, float]] = {}
130	    for group_name, promos in named_groups.items():
131	        best_p: Promotion | None = None
132	        best_d: float = 0.0
133	        for p in promos:
134	            d = _calc_discount(p, subtotal, course_ids)
135	            if d > best_d:
136	                best_d = d
137	                best_p = p
138	        if best_p and best_d > 0:
139	            best_per_group[group_name] = (best_p, best_d)
140	
141	    # --- Step 4: stack best-per-named-group + optional best-null ---
142	    # Build candidate combination: one from each named group (stackable) + best null
143	    candidate: list[AppliedPromotion] = []
144	    for group_name, (p, d) in best_per_group.items():
145	        candidate.append(AppliedPromotion(p, d, _build_explanation(p, d)))
146	    if best_null and best_null_discount > 0:
147	        candidate.append(AppliedPromotion(best_null, best_null_discount, _build_explanation(best_null, best_null_discount)))
148	
149	    candidate_total = sum(a.discount_amount for a in candidate)
150	    if candidate_total > best_total:
151	        best_total = candidate_total
152	        best = candidate
153	
154	    # Cap: discount cannot exceed subtotal
155	    if best_total > subtotal:
156	        # Scale down proportionally
157	        factor = subtotal / best_total
158	        best = [
159	            AppliedPromotion(a.promotion, round(a.discount_amount * factor, 2), a.explanation)
160	            for a in best
161	        ]
162	
163	    return best
164

2	
3	import csv
4	import hashlib
5	import hmac
6	import os
7	import uuid
8	from datetime import UTC, date, datetime, timedelta
9	
10	from sqlalchemy import and_, select
11	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
12	
13	from app.core.audit import log_audit
14	from app.core.config import settings
15	from app.core.exceptions import ConflictError, NotFoundError, UnprocessableError
16	from app.modules.bookings.models import Booking, BookingStatus
17	from app.modules.checkout.models import Order, OrderItem, OrderStatus
18	from app.modules.payments.models import (
19	    Payment, PaymentStatus, ReconciliationExport, Refund, RefundStatus,
20	)
21	from app.modules.payments.schemas import (
22	    PaymentCallbackPayload, PaymentResponse, ReconciliationExportResponse,
23	    RefundCreate, RefundResponse,
24	)
25	
26	
27	def _verify_signature(payload: PaymentCallbackPayload) -> bool:
28	    message = f"{payload.terminal_ref}{payload.amount}{payload.timestamp}"
29	    expected = hmac.new(
30	        settings.PAYMENT_SIGNATURE_SECRET.encode(),
31	        message.encode(),
32	        hashlib.sha256,
33	    ).hexdigest()
34	    return hmac.compare_digest(expected, payload.signature)
35	
36	
37	class PaymentService:
38	    def __init__(self, db: AsyncSession) -> None:
39	        self._db = db
40	
41	    async def handle_callback(self, payload: PaymentCallbackPayload) -> PaymentResponse:
42	        sig_ok = _verify_signature(payload)
43	
44	        result = await self._db.execute(
45	            select(Payment).where(Payment.order_id == payload.order_id)
46	        )
47	        payment = result.scalar_one_or_none()
48	        if not payment:
49	            raise NotFoundError("Payment")
50	
51	        payment.terminal_ref = payload.terminal_ref
52	        payment.callback_received_at = datetime.now(UTC)
53	        payment.signature_verified = sig_ok
54	        payment.raw_callback = payload.model_dump(mode="json")
55	
56	        if sig_ok:
57	            payment.status = PaymentStatus.completed
58	            # Mark order as paid
59	            order_result = await self._db.execute(select(Order).where(Order.id == payload.order_id))
60	            order = order_result.scalar_one_or_none()

50	        assert resp.json()["username"] == "me_user"

28	    r"^(?=.*[a-z])(?=.*[A-Z])(?=.*\d)(?=.*[!@#$%^&*()_+\-=\[\]{};':\"\\|,.<>/?]).{12,}$"
29	)
30	
31	
32	def validate_password_complexity(password: str) -> None:
33	    """Raise ValueError if password does not meet complexity rules."""
34	    if not _PASSWORD_RE.match(password):
35	        raise ValueError(
36	            "Password must be at least 12 characters and contain uppercase, "
37	            "lowercase, digit, and special character."
38	        )
